This table presents the descriptive statistics for the LLM-assigned scores aggregated at the AI Surrogate condition level: the mean of the three repeated scoring calls per AI surrogate, per experimental cell.This granular view is essential because the discriminating power (SD) and the central tendency (M) of the LLM are expected to shift as a function of the textual information provided

```{r}
#| label: tbl-llm-scores-by-condition
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "LLM-Scored Need Satisfaction: Descriptive Statistics per Condition"

# 1. Prepare Table Data (Layer B) ----------------------------------------------
# Use  pre-calculated llm_cond_stats
tbl_llm_cond_display <- llm_cond_stats |>
  # Select identifying factors and the target statistics
  dplyr::select(condition, subscale, m, sd, min, max) |>
  # Pivot to a wide format for a clear APA-style comparison matrix
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = subscale,
    values_from = c(m, sd, min, max),
    names_glue = "{subscale}_{.value}"
  ) |>
  # Order columns by the NEEDS constant: Autonomy, Competence, Relatedness [cite: 31]
  dplyr::select(
    Condition = condition,
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  )

# 2. Render APA Table (Layer B) ------------------------------------------------
tbl_llm_cond_display |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  # Format to 2 decimal places per APA 7th edition standards [cite: 27]
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    Autonomy_m     = "M", Autonomy_sd     = "SD", Autonomy_min   = "Min", Autonomy_max   = "Max",
    Competence_m   = "M", Competence_sd   = "SD", Competence_min = "Min", Competence_max = "Max",
    Relatedness_m  = "M", Relatedness_sd  = "SD", Relatedness_min = "Min", Relatedness_max = "Max"
  ) |>
  # Add hierarchical headers for the psychological dimensions
  add_header_row(
    values = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1, 4, 4, 4)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(
    paste0("N = ", n_distinct(d_conditions$uuid),
           " AI surrogates. SD represents between-surrogate variability of AI Surrogate-level means for LLM scores. Min/Max are observed score extremes per condition.")
  ) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-llm-scores-by-condition-iqr
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "LLM-Scored Need Satisfaction: IQR and Range per Condition"

d_conditions |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m),
    names_to  = "subscale",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(subscale = case_match(subscale,
    "llm_aut_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
    "llm_com_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
    "llm_rel_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
  )) |>
  group_by(condition, subscale) |>
  summarise(
    iqr   = IQR(score),
    range = max(score) - min(score),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from  = subscale,
    values_from = c(iqr, range),
    names_glue  = "{subscale}_{.value}"
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    Condition = condition,
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    Autonomy_iqr      = "IQR", Autonomy_range    = "Range",
    Competence_iqr    = "IQR", Competence_range  = "Range",
    Relatedness_iqr   = "IQR", Relatedness_range = "Range"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1, 2, 2, 2)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(
    paste0("N = ", n_distinct(d_conditions$uuid),
           " AI surrogates. IQR = interquartile range (Q3 − Q1). Range = maximum − minimum (observed score extremes) per condition.")
  ) |>
  autofit()
```


### 6.5.1b LLM Scores Distribution Statistics (Kurtosis, Skewness) per Condition


Interpretation Kurtosis
- Excess kurtosis near 0: roughly normal tail behavior
- Positive excess kurtosis: more outlier-prone, heavier tails
- Negative excess kurtosis: flatter / lighter-tailed / more bounded

Interpretation Skewness
- Around 0: little asymmetry
- Larger positive values: increasing right-tail pull; the mean is often above the median
- Larger negative values: increasing left-tail pull; the mean is often below the median

```{r}
#| label: tbl-llm-scores-distrib
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "LLM-Scored Need Satisfaction: Distribution Statistics per Condition (Median, Mode, Skewness, Kurtosis)"

mode_val <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

words_per_cond <- d_conditions |>
  group_by(condition) |>
  summarise(m_words = round(mean(actual_words), 1), .groups = "drop")

d_conditions |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m),
    names_to  = "subscale",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(subscale = case_match(subscale,
    "llm_aut_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
    "llm_com_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
    "llm_rel_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
  )) |>
  group_by(condition, subscale) |>
  summarise(
    mdn  = round(median(score), 2),
    mode = round(mode_val(score), 2),
    skew = round(psych::skew(score), 2),
    kurt = round(psych::kurtosi(score), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from  = subscale,
    values_from = c(mdn, mode, skew, kurt),
    names_glue  = "{subscale}_{.value}"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(words_per_cond, by = "condition") |>
  dplyr::select(
    Condition = condition,
    m_words,
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    starts_with(TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    m_words         = "M Words",
    Autonomy_mdn    = "Mdn",  Autonomy_mode    = "Mode",  Autonomy_skew    = "Skew",  Autonomy_kurt    = "Kurt",
    Competence_mdn  = "Mdn",  Competence_mode  = "Mode",  Competence_skew  = "Skew",  Competence_kurt  = "Kurt",
    Relatedness_mdn = "Mdn",  Relatedness_mode = "Mode",  Relatedness_skew = "Skew",  Relatedness_kurt = "Kurt"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1, 1, 4, 4, 4)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "N = ", n_distinct(d_conditions$uuid), " AI surrogates. ",
    "M Words = mean observed word count per condition. ",
    "Mdn = median; Mode = most frequent observed value; ",
    "Skew = Fisher's skewness coefficient (g1); Kurt = excess kurtosis (g2)."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```


### 6.5.2 LLM Scores Boxplots

To assess the distributional properties of the LLM-assigned scores, apply the same visualization strategy used for the questionnaire composite scores. This consistency allows for a direct comparison of score density, central tendency, and potential outliers between the human-origin "ground truth" and the AI-generated inferences.

```{r}
#| label: fig-llm-condition-boxplot
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Spread of LLM-Scored Needs across 3x3 Experimental Conditions"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8

# LAYER B: Faceted Boxplots
d_conditions |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m), 
    names_to = "subscale", 
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(subscale = case_match(subscale,
    "llm_aut_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
    "llm_com_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
    "llm_rel_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(target_words), y = score, fill = factor(text_count))) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2, outlier.size = 0.5) +
  # Facet by Subscale and Number of Texts to match the RQ2 structure
  facet_grid(subscale ~ text_count, labeller = label_both) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = COL_AI_TEXTS) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5) +
  labs(
    title = "LLM Score Distribution by Information Quantity",
    subtitle = "Faceted by Psychological Need and Number of Texts",
    x = "Target Word Count (Length)",
    y = "Mean LLM Score (1–5)",
    fill = "Texts (n)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    text = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)
  )
```

### 6.5.3 LLM Scores Histograms


```{r}
#| label: fig-llm-condition-hist
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Frequency Distribution of LLM Scores across the 3x3 Factorial Design"
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 10

# LAYER B: Faceted Histograms
d_hist_vlines <- d_conditions |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m),
    names_to = "subscale",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(subscale = case_match(subscale,
    "llm_aut_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
    "llm_com_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
    "llm_rel_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
  )) |>
  group_by(condition, subscale) |>
  summarise(mean_score = mean(score), median_score = median(score), .groups = "drop")

d_conditions |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m),
    names_to = "subscale",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(subscale = case_match(subscale,
    "llm_aut_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
    "llm_com_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
    "llm_rel_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = score, fill = subscale)) +
  # Using binwidth 0.33 to align with the discrete sum/3 math of the means
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.33, color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(data = d_hist_vlines, aes(xintercept = mean_score, color = "Mean"),
             linetype = "solid", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(data = d_hist_vlines, aes(xintercept = median_score, color = "Median"),
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  # Full 3x3 grid for one subscale at a time, or condensed view
  facet_grid(condition ~ subscale) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = COL_AI_LLM) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = NULL,
    values = c("Mean" = "#CC0000", "Median" = "#DC7D71"),
    breaks = c("Mean", "Median"),
    guide  = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = c("solid", "dashed"), linewidth = 0.7))
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, 5)) +  # zoom only — keeps bin edges aligned with the data
  labs(
    title = "Score Frequency per Factorial Cell",
    x = "Mean LLM Score (1–5)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    text = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 8)
  )
```

### 6.5.4 LLM Scores Histograms: Density plots

- Discriminating Power: A "flatter" and "wider" density curve indicates higher between-surrogate variability (SD), suggesting the LLM is better at differentiating between AI Surrogates in that specific condition.

- Instructional Fidelity: The peaks of these curves should align with the global means reported in Table 6.5.1.

- Ceiling Effects: If the curves are heavily skewed toward 5.0, it indicates a "liberal-prosocial bias" or "ceiling effect" that may attenuate the correlations analyzed in RQ2b.

```{r}
#| label: fig-llm-condition-density
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Density Distribution of LLM Scores across the 3x3 Factorial Design"
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 10
#| warning: false

# LAYER B: Faceted Density Plots
d_conditions |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m), 
    names_to = "subscale", 
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(subscale = case_match(subscale,
    "llm_aut_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
    "llm_com_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
    "llm_rel_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = score, fill = subscale, color = subscale)) +
  # Using geom_density to visualize the underlying probability distribution
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.6) +
  # Facet grid matches the experimental structure: Need x Condition
  facet_grid(condition ~ subscale) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = COL_AI_LLM) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_LLM) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5) +
  labs(
    title = "LLM Score Density per Factorial Cell",
    subtitle = "Smoothed probability distributions of AI Surrogate-level means",
    x = "Mean LLM Score (1–5)",
    y = "Density",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    text = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = 9),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

### 6.5.5 LLM vs. Questionnaire Score Density per Need and Condition

Kernel density curves overlay LLM scores (blue) and questionnaire scores (red) for each need dimension and experimental condition. Because questionnaire scores are person-level constants, their distribution is identical across conditions — differences across rows reflect only LLM score changes. Rug marks show individual observations.

```{r}
#| label: fig-density-llm-vs-q
#| fig-cap: "Kernel density estimates of LLM scores (blue) and questionnaire scores (red) per need dimension and condition. Rug marks indicate individual observations. Questionnaire score distributions are identical across conditions."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 22
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| warning: false

# LLM scores: one row per persona × condition
d_llm_long <- d_conditions |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m),
    names_to  = "need",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    need   = case_match(need,
      "llm_aut_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      "llm_com_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      "llm_rel_m" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    source = "LLM Score"
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(condition, need, score, source)

# Q scores: person-level — repeated across all conditions for visual overlay
d_q_long <- d_persona |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(q_aut_mean, q_com_mean, q_rel_mean),
    names_to  = "need",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    need   = case_match(need,
      "q_aut_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      "q_com_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      "q_rel_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    source = "Questionnaire Score"
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(need, score, source) |>
  tidyr::crossing(condition = CONDITIONS)

d_density_overlay <- bind_rows(d_llm_long, d_q_long) |>
  mutate(
    need      = factor(need, levels = NEEDS),
    condition = factor(condition, levels = CONDITIONS),
    source    = factor(source, levels = c("LLM Score", "Questionnaire Score"))
  )

ggplot(d_density_overlay, aes(x = score, fill = source, color = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_rug(alpha = 0.25, sides = "b") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 3, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_grid(condition ~ need) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c("LLM Score" = COL_AI_SINGLE, "Questionnaire Score" = COL_HU_SINGLE)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("LLM Score" = COL_AI_SINGLE, "Questionnaire Score" = COL_HU_SINGLE)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 5), breaks = 1:5) +
  labs(
    title = "LLM vs. Questionnaire Score Density per Need and Condition",
    x     = "Score (1–5)",
    y     = "Density",
    fill  = "Score Type",
    color = "Score Type"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
```

## 6.6 Comparison

```{r}
#| label: tbl-llm-vs-q-overall
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "LLM and Questionnaire Need Satisfaction: Grand Means Across All Conditions"

llm_overall <- llm_cond_stats |>
  group_by(subscale) |>
  summarise(
    llm_m  = mean(m),
    llm_sd = mean(sd),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

q_overall <- q_comp_stats |>
  dplyr::select(subscale, q_m = m, q_sd = sd)

llm_overall |>
  left_join(q_overall, by = "subscale") |>
  rename(!!LABEL_NEEDS := subscale) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    llm_m = "M", llm_sd = "SD",
    q_m   = "M", q_sd   = "SD"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "LLM Scores", "Questionnaire"),
    colwidths = c(1, 2, 2)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(
    paste0("N = ", n_distinct(d_conditions$uuid),
           " AI surrogates. LLM M and SD are grand means averaged across all 9 experimental conditions ",
           "(3 text lengths × 3 text counts). Questionnaire M and SD are composite scores ",
           "(mean of 4 items per dimension) collapsed across all AI surrogates.")
  ) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-llm-vs-q-means
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Mean Need Satisfaction by Condition and Score Source"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8


#COL_YEL = "#DDA613"
#COL_AI_SHADES = c("#1F5A71", "#2085AB", "#489CC1", "#6DBCE0", "#9ecae1","#FFFFFF")
#COL_HU_SHADES = c("#BE2A16", "#CE5545", "#DC7D71", "#E9A39B", "#F5C9C5","#FFFFFF")

plot_data_comp <- bind_rows(
  llm_cond_stats |>
    dplyr::select(subscale, label = condition, m, sd) |>
    mutate(source = "LLM", label = as.character(label)),
  q_comp_stats |>
    dplyr::select(subscale, m, sd) |>
    mutate(source = "Questionnaire", label = "Questionnaire")
) |>
  mutate(
    label    = factor(label, levels = c(CONDITIONS, "Questionnaire")),
    subscale = factor(subscale, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL))
  )

q_hlines <- q_comp_stats |>
  dplyr::select(subscale, q_m = m, q_sd = sd) |>
  mutate(subscale = factor(subscale, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)))

ggplot(plot_data_comp, aes(x = label, y = m, fill = source)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72) +
  geom_col(aes(y = sd), width = 0.12, fill = COL_AI_SHADES[3], inherit.aes = TRUE) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = m - sd, ymax = m + sd),
    width     = 0.3,
    linewidth = 0.45,
    colour    = COL_YEL
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    data      = q_hlines,
    aes(yintercept = q_m),
    linetype  = "dotted",
    colour    = COL_HU_SHADES[1],
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    data      = q_hlines,
    aes(yintercept = q_sd),
    linetype  = "dotted",
    colour    = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~subscale, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("LLM" = COL_AI_SINGLE, "Questionnaire" = COL_HU_SHADES[2]),
    name   = "Score source"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 4.5, 0.5)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 4.5)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Mean Need Satisfaction by Condition and Score Source",
    subtitle = "Error bars indicate ±1 SD across AI surrogates",
    x        = "Condition",
    y        = "Mean Score (1–5)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text             = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)
  )
```

# 7. Prerequisites & Reliability

## 7.1 Questionnaire Reliability (Cronbach, Omega)

7.1.1 Questionnaire (Q) Item-Total Correlation Audit
This validation step identifies items that conflict with the overall subscale direction. If an item is negatively correlated, it suggests the AI surrogate interpreted the prompt's polarity incorrectly for that specific question

For a two‑way mixed‑effects model with fixed items/raters, consistency type, and mean of k measurements (ICC(3,k)), Cronbach’s alpha is mathematically equivalent to that ICC.

```{r}
#| label: validate-q-item-correlations
#| echo: true

# 1. Diagnostic Function (Layer C) ---------------------------------------------
audit_item_correlations <- function(data, prefix) {
  # Select the 4 items for the subscale [cite: 132, 174]
  items <- data |> dplyr::select(starts_with(prefix))
  
  # Calculate the correlation of each item with the total of the others
  # This avoids the deprecated response.frequency calls in psych::alpha
  item_names <- colnames(items)
  
  results <- purrr::map_df(item_names, function(item) {
    others <- items |> dplyr::select(-all_of(item))
    total_others <- rowMeans(others) # Use mean of other 3 items
    
    # Calculate point-biserial/Pearson correlation
    r_drop <- cor(items[[item]], total_others, use = "complete.obs")
    
    tibble::tibble(
      Item = item,
      r_drop = round(r_drop, 3),
      Status = if_else(r_drop < 0, "NEGATIVE CORRELATION", "OK")
    )
  })
  
  return(results)
}

# 2. Execution (Layer C) -------------------------------------------------------
# Use d_persona (N = 394 unique personas) 
q_item_audit <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  audit_item_correlations(d_persona, "q_aut"),
  audit_item_correlations(d_persona, "q_com"),
  audit_item_correlations(d_persona, "q_rel")
)

# 3. Output Filtered Culprits --------------------------------------------------
q_culprits <- q_item_audit |> dplyr::filter(Status == "NEGATIVE CORRELATION")

if (nrow(q_culprits) > 0) {
  # Hard failure gate — negative item-total correlation invalidates the subscale composite
  print(q_culprits)
  stop("CRITICAL: The following items correlate negatively with their subscales (see above).")
} else {
  # Clean status line for Quarto render
  cat("Success: All Q items correlate positively with their respective subscales.\n")
}
```

7.1.2 Cronbach and Omega

```{r}
#| label: tbl-questionnaire-reliability
#| echo: true
#| tbl-cap: "Internal Consistency and Scoring of Questionnaire (Q) Subscales"

#  Alpha_perf and Alpha_psych handle missing data and item scaling differently
# 
# Critical Parameters Explained
# check.keys = TRUE (psych): This is the most critical setting. It prevents a "false low" reliability score. If the AI generator created a AI Surrogate that interprets an autonomy item inversely, psych will flip it to match the scale's direction.
# 
# nfactors = 1 (omega): Because reliability for individual subscales (e.g., just Competence),  instruct the model to look for exactly one general factor.
# 
# warnings = FALSE: During empirical data collection, small sample sizes or low variance can trigger mathematical warnings about "singular matrices." Since we use an automated pipeline, we suppress these to keep the Quarto render clean, but we monitor the actual coefficients for validity.

# 1. Scoring Logic (Layer C) ---------------------------------------------------

# Explicitly define keys: 1 indicates positive loading, -1 for reverse-coded.
# Based on your audit, all current items are positively keyed (1).
q_keys_list <- list(
Autonomy    = c("q_aut_1", "q_aut_2", "q_aut_3", "q_aut_4"),
  Competence  = c("q_com_1", "q_com_2", "q_com_3", "q_com_4"),
  Relatedness = c("q_rel_1", "q_rel_2", "q_rel_3", "q_rel_4")
)

# Execute scoreItems
# select(q_aut_1:q_rel_4) ensures we only process the 12 raw integer items.
q_scoring <- psych::scoreItems(
  keys    = q_keys_list, 
  items   = d_persona |> dplyr::select(q_aut_1:q_rel_4),
  totals  = FALSE, # Returns Means (1-5) instead of Sums (4-20)
  missing = TRUE,  # Uses available items if some are NA (imputed by item mean)
  min = 1, max = 5
)

# 2. Extract Reliability Metrics (Layer B) -------------------------------------

# q_scoring$alpha contains the raw alpha for each scale
# q_scoring$item.cor contains the item-total correlations (r.drop)
rel_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  Dimension = NEEDS,
  Items     = 4,
  Alpha     = as.numeric(q_scoring$alpha)
)

# 3. Render APA Table (Layer B) ------------------------------------------------

rel_summary |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(j = 3, digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    Dimension = LABEL_NEEDS,
    Alpha     = "\u03b1"
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(
    paste0("N = ", q_scoring$n.obs, " unique AI surrogates. ",
           "Calculated via psych::scoreItems with explicit positive keying.")
  ) |>
  autofit()
```



Alternative calculation: Cronbach + Omega

```{r}
#| label: tbl-reliability-omega
#| include: false
#| cache: true
#| cache.extra: !expr file.mtime(data_path)

# Calculate Omega for each subscale (plot = FALSE suppresses path diagram rendering)
omega_aut <- omega(d_persona |> dplyr::select(q_aut_1:q_aut_4), plot = FALSE)
omega_com <- omega(d_persona |> dplyr::select(q_com_1:q_com_4), plot = FALSE)
omega_rel <- omega(d_persona |> dplyr::select(q_rel_1:q_rel_4), plot = FALSE)

reliability_summary <- data.frame(
  Scale = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
  Alpha = c(
    performance::cronbachs_alpha(d_persona |> dplyr::select(q_aut_1:q_aut_4)),
    performance::cronbachs_alpha(d_persona |> dplyr::select(q_com_1:q_com_4)),
    performance::cronbachs_alpha(d_persona |> dplyr::select(q_rel_1:q_rel_4))
  ),
  Omega = c(omega_aut$omega.tot, omega_com$omega.tot, omega_rel$omega.tot)
)

print(reliability_summary)

```


## 7.2 LLM Test-Retest Reliability — Average Measures ICC(2,1) (Level: Conditions)

To assess the stability of the LLM scoring engine, calculate  Intraclass Correlation Coefficient (ICC) across the three repeated scoring iterations with a two-way random-effects model with absolute agreement ($ICC_{2,1}$). This acknowledges that each API call is a random draw from the LLM's stochastic distribution.

Interpretation: An ICC of 0.00 means that there is zero absolute agreement between the three stochastic API calls.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-llm-icc-results
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: true
#| cache.extra: !expr file.mtime(data_path)
#| tbl-cap: "LLM Scoring Reliability: Two-Way Random Effects, Absolute Agreement (ICC 2,1)"

# 1. ICC Helper Function (Layer C) ---------------------------------------------
calc_condition_icc <- function(sub_data, score_var) {
  # Pivot to wide: personas as rows, the 3 random draws (repeats) as columns
  wide_reps <- sub_data |>
    dplyr::select(uuid, repeat_id, !!sym(score_var)) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from  = repeat_id,
      values_from = !!sym(score_var),
      names_prefix = "draw_"
    )

  # Validate no personas were silently dropped during pivot
  n_expected <- dplyr::n_distinct(sub_data$uuid)
  if (nrow(wide_reps) != n_expected) {
    warning("ICC pivot: expected ", n_expected, " rows, got ", nrow(wide_reps),
            " for score_var = ", score_var,
            ". Effective N is reduced; ICC CIs will be wider.")
  }

  wide_reps <- wide_reps |> dplyr::select(starts_with("draw_"))

  # Execute ICC. Note: 'missing = TRUE' handles any rare API timeouts
  # We do not pass model/type here; psych::ICC computes all variants.
  icc_full_output <- psych::ICC(wide_reps, missing = TRUE)
  
  # Extract ICC2 (Random Effects, Single Measure) per Analytical Strategy 
  res_row <- icc_full_output$results |> 
    dplyr::filter(type == "ICC2") |>
    dplyr::select(value = ICC, lo = `lower bound`, hi = `upper bound`)
  
  return(res_row)
}

# 2. Execution Loop (Layer C) --------------------------------------------------
# Reusing your global variables: CONDITIONS and NEEDS [cite: 29, 31]
icc_grid <- expand.grid(
  cond = CONDITIONS,
  need = c("llm_aut", "llm_com", "llm_rel"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

tbl_icc_raw <- icc_grid |>
  purrr::pmap_dfr(function(cond, need) {
    sub_df <- d |> dplyr::filter(condition == cond)
    stats  <- calc_condition_icc(sub_df, need)
    
    tibble::tibble(
      Condition = cond,
      Need      = need,
      ICC       = stats$value,
      CI_lo     = stats$lo,
      CI_hi     = stats$hi
    )
  })

# 3. Format for Thesis (Layer B) -----------------------------------------------
tbl_icc_display <- tbl_icc_raw |>
  mutate(
    # Mapping to your global TABLE_LABEL_* constants [cite: 31]
    Need = case_match(Need,
      "llm_aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      "llm_com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      "llm_rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    # APA 7 formatted string [cite: 27]
    Result = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", ICC, CI_lo, CI_hi)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Condition, Need, Result) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Need, values_from = Result)

# 4. Render Table (Layer B) ----------------------------------------------------
tbl_icc_display |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_header_row(
    values = c("", "ICC(2,1) [95% CI] (Two-Way Random, Absolute Agreement)"),
    colwidths = c(1, 3)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(
    paste0("Reliability of single scoring draws (ICC2) reflecting agreement across ",
           "3 stochastic API calls per condition. Calculated via psych::ICC with ",
           "missing = TRUE (listwise deletion on the 3-draw wide matrix); ",
           "effective N per cell may be below the reported sample size if any repeat was missing.")
  ) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: validate-icc-thresholds
#| include: false

# Structural Check (Layer C)
low_reliability <- tbl_icc_raw |> filter(ICC < 0.75)

if (nrow(low_reliability) > 0) {
  message("--- DEBUG: Reliability Alert ---")
  message("The following conditions show < 'Good' reliability (ICC < .75):")
  print(low_reliability)
} else {
  message("Success: All conditions show 'Good' or 'Excellent' reliability (ICC > .75).")
}
```

```{r}
#| label: debug-icc-variance
#| include: false 

# 1. Decompose Variance for a single Need (e.g., Autonomy) ---------------------
# Pre-compute within-SD per condition (mean of per-persona repeat SDs)
within_sd_by_cond <- d |>
  group_by(uuid, condition) |>
  summarise(sd_rep = sd(llm_aut, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(condition) |>
  summarise(within_sd = mean(sd_rep, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

diag_variance <- d |>
  group_by(condition) |>
  summarise(
    between_sd = sd(llm_aut_m, na.rm = TRUE),  # Signal: persona differences
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(within_sd_by_cond, by = "condition")  # Noise: within-persona repeat variation

#cat("Variance Decomposition (Autonomy):\n")
#print(diag_variance)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-icc-forest
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: |
#|   _LLM Test-Retest Reliability: ICC(2,1) with 95% CI by Condition and Need Dimension._
#|   _Dashed lines mark conventional "good" (.75) and "excellent" (.90) thresholds._

fig_icc_forest <- tbl_icc_raw |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_match(Need,
      "llm_aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      "llm_com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      "llm_rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Condition = factor(Condition, levels = rev(CONDITIONS)),
    # Manual vertical offset per need so points don't overlap
    y_num = as.numeric(Condition) + case_match(Need,
      TABLE_LABEL_AUT ~ -0.22,
      TABLE_LABEL_COM ~  0.00,
      TABLE_LABEL_REL ~  0.22
    )
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = ICC, y = y_num, colour = Need)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.75, 0.90), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = CI_lo, xmax = CI_hi), height = 0.15) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_LLM) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90, 1.00),
                       labels = c("0", ".25", ".50", ".75", ".90", "1.00")) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq_along(CONDITIONS), labels = rev(CONDITIONS)) +
    labs(x = "ICC(2,1)", y = NULL, colour = "Need") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
    theme(
      legend.position    = "top",
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
    )

fig_icc_forest
```

## 7.2b LLM Scoring Reliability — Average Measures ICC(2,k=3)

ICC(2,1) above characterises the reliability of a *single* LLM scoring call. Because every downstream analysis (RQ2a SD, RQ2b correlations, RQ3) uses the **mean of k = 3 calls**, the directly relevant quantity is ICC(2,k=3): the expected reliability of that mean. Via the Spearman–Brown prophecy formula, ICC(2,k) = k × ICC(2,1) / [1 + (k − 1) × ICC(2,1)]. `psych::ICC()` computes this as `ICC2k` in the same call, so no additional API data are needed.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-llm-icc2k-results
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| cache: true
#| cache.extra: !expr file.mtime(data_path)
#| tbl-cap: "LLM Scoring Reliability: Two-Way Random Effects, Absolute Agreement, Average of k = 3 Measures (ICC(2,3))"

# 1. ICC2k Helper Function (Layer C) -------------------------------------------
calc_condition_icc2k <- function(sub_data, score_var) {
  wide_reps <- sub_data |>
    dplyr::select(uuid, repeat_id, !!sym(score_var)) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from  = repeat_id,
      values_from = !!sym(score_var),
      names_prefix = "draw_"
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(starts_with("draw_"))

  icc_full_output <- psych::ICC(wide_reps, missing = TRUE)

  # Extract ICC2k (Two-Way Random, Absolute Agreement, Average of k Measures)
  res_row <- icc_full_output$results |>
    dplyr::filter(type == "ICC2k") |>
    dplyr::select(value = ICC, lo = `lower bound`, hi = `upper bound`)

  return(res_row)
}

# 2. Execution Loop (Layer C) --------------------------------------------------
icc2k_grid <- expand.grid(
  cond = CONDITIONS,
  need = c("llm_aut", "llm_com", "llm_rel"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

tbl_icc2k_raw <- icc2k_grid |>
  purrr::pmap_dfr(function(cond, need) {
    sub_df <- d |> dplyr::filter(condition == cond)
    stats  <- calc_condition_icc2k(sub_df, need)

    tibble::tibble(
      Condition = cond,
      Need      = need,
      ICC       = stats$value,
      CI_lo     = stats$lo,
      CI_hi     = stats$hi
    )
  })

# 3. Format for Thesis (Layer B) -----------------------------------------------
tbl_icc2k_display <- tbl_icc2k_raw |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_match(Need,
      "llm_aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      "llm_com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      "llm_rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Result = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", ICC, CI_lo, CI_hi)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Condition, Need, Result) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Need, values_from = Result)

# 4. Render Table (Layer B) ----------------------------------------------------
tbl_icc2k_display |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_header_row(
    values = c("", "ICC(2,3) [95% CI] (Two-Way Random, Absolute Agreement, k = 3)"),
    colwidths = c(1, 3)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(
    paste0("Reliability of the mean of k = 3 scoring calls per condition (ICC2k). ",
           "This is the ICC that corresponds to the measurement actually used in all ",
           "downstream analyses. Calculated via psych::ICC(); equivalent to the ",
           "Spearman–Brown adjustment of ICC(2,1) with k = 3.")
  ) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-icc2k-forest
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: |
#|   _LLM Scoring Reliability: ICC(2,3) with 95% CI by Condition and Need Dimension._
#|   _Dashed lines mark conventional "good" (.75) and "excellent" (.90) thresholds._

tbl_icc2k_raw |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_match(Need,
      "llm_aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      "llm_com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      "llm_rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Condition = factor(Condition, levels = rev(CONDITIONS)),
    y_num = as.numeric(Condition) + case_match(Need,
      TABLE_LABEL_AUT ~ -0.22,
      TABLE_LABEL_COM ~  0.00,
      TABLE_LABEL_REL ~  0.22
    )
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = ICC, y = y_num, colour = Need)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.75, 0.90), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = CI_lo, xmax = CI_hi), height = 0.15) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_LLM) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90, 1.00),
                       labels = c("0", ".25", ".50", ".75", ".90", "1.00")) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq_along(CONDITIONS), labels = rev(CONDITIONS)) +
    labs(x = "ICC(2,3)", y = NULL, colour = "Need") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
    theme(
      legend.position    = "top",
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
    )
```

## 7.3 Benchmarks & Diagnostics (NOT YET USED).

7.3.1 Latent Structure of LLM Scores

The Analytical Strategy proposed a CFA on LLM scores to check whether the three need dimensions are distinct or collapse into a single valence factor. However, with only one LLM score per need (llm_aut, llm_com, llm_rel), the model is just-identified (df = 0) and produces trivially perfect fit regardless of the data. The diagnostic question — whether inter-need correlations exceed the SDT meta-analytic ceiling (r > .80; Van den Broeck et al., 2016) — is already answered by @fig-rq2b-crosscor-heatmap, which shows within-LLM inter-need correlations across all nine conditions.

7.3.2 Latent Structure of Questionnaire Scores

We fit a 1-factor (general valence) and 3-factor (SDT) CFA on the 12 questionnaire items, one row per persona, and compare fit via LRT.

```{r}
#| label: validate-q-cfa
#| include: true
#| message: false

# --- Models ---
q_model_1f <- '
  General =~ q_aut_1 + q_aut_2 + q_aut_3 + q_aut_4 +
             q_com_1 + q_com_2 + q_com_3 + q_com_4 +
             q_rel_1 + q_rel_2 + q_rel_3 + q_rel_4
'
q_model_3f <- '
  Autonomy    =~ q_aut_1 + q_aut_2 + q_aut_3 + q_aut_4
  Competence  =~ q_com_1 + q_com_2 + q_com_3 + q_com_4
  Relatedness =~ q_rel_1 + q_rel_2 + q_rel_3 + q_rel_4
'

fit_1f <- cfa(q_model_1f, data = d_persona)
fit_3f <- cfa(q_model_3f, data = d_persona)

# --- Fit indices ---
idx_names <- c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "rmsea", "srmr")
fi_1f <- fitmeasures(fit_1f, idx_names)
fi_3f <- fitmeasures(fit_3f, idx_names)

cfa_comparison <- rbind(
  `1-factor` = round(fi_1f, 3),
  `3-factor` = round(fi_3f, 3)
)

# --- LRT: 3-factor vs 1-factor ---
lrt <- lavTestLRT(fit_1f, fit_3f)

cat("Model comparison (N =", nrow(d_persona), "):
")
print(cfa_comparison)
cat("
Likelihood Ratio Test (3-factor vs 1-factor):
")
print(lrt)
```

7.3.3 Descriptive Benchmarks (Olafsen vs. Surrogate)

```{r}
#| label: tbl-norm-comparison
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Comparison of AI Surrogate Scores with Rescaled Human Norms"

# 1. Prepare Data using existing objects from 6.4 [cite: 207, 208]
tbl_norm_comp <- norm_summary |>
  left_join(q_comp_stats, by = "subscale") |>
  mutate(
    diff_m  = round(m  - norm_m,  2),
    diff_sd = round(sd - norm_sd, 2),
    m       = round(m,  2),
    sd      = round(sd, 2)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    Subscale       = subscale,
    `Norm M`       = norm_m,
    `Norm SD†`     = norm_sd,
    `Surrogate M`  = m,
    `Surrogate SD` = sd,
    `Δ M`          = diff_m,
    `Δ SD`         = diff_sd
  )

# 2. Render with style_apa7 and appropriate headers [cite: 209, 210]
tbl_norm_comp |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Subscale      = LABEL_NEEDS,
    `Norm M`      = "Norm M",
    `Surrogate M` = "Surrogate M",
    `Δ M`         = "Δ M",
    `Δ SD`        = "Δ SD"
  ) |>
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  add_apa_note(
    paste0("Norms from Olafsen et al. (2021), Sample 4 (N = 393), rescaled from 1–7 to 1–5 scale. ",
           "AI Surrogate N = ", nrow(d_persona), ". † Norm SDs derived from item-level SEs.")
  ) |>
  autofit()
```


```{r}
#| label: setup-rq2-bootstrap
#| include: false
#| cache: true
#| cache.extra: !expr list(file.mtime(data_path), BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)


# ── Wide data: one row per persona ───────────────────────────────────────────
# Pivot d_conditions to wide so each condition LLM score is its own column.
# This makes the persona the resampling unit, which is required: condition-level
# values (SDs, correlations) are not independent, but bootstrapping personas is
# a valid nonparametric approach (see Analytical Strategy §RQ2 independence
# constraint and 70_Simulation.qmd §5).

# Primary source is d_conditions, i.e. each condition in each row with multiple personas
# at the end, each personas has onle one row with all conditions. 

wide_rq2 <- d_conditions |>
  dplyr::select(uuid, condition, llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from  = condition,
    values_from = c(llm_aut_m, llm_com_m, llm_rel_m)
  ) |>
  left_join(
    d_persona |> dplyr::select(uuid, q_aut_mean, q_com_mean, q_rel_mean),
    by = "uuid"
  )

# checks if now each persona occurs once per row
stopifnot(
  "wide_rq2: one row per persona required" =
    nrow(wide_rq2) == n_distinct(d_conditions$uuid),
  "wide_rq2: no duplicate uuids" =
    !anyDuplicated(wide_rq2$uuid)
)

condition_lookup <- tibble::tibble( # keyed on condition name — join on "condition" column, never rely on position
  condition    = CONDITIONS,
  target_words = rep(as.integer(WORD_LENGTH_VARIANTS), each = length(N_TEXTS_VARIANTS)),
  text_count   = rep(N_TEXTS_VARIANTS, times = length(WORD_LENGTH_VARIANTS))
)

aut_cols <- paste0("llm_aut_m_", CONDITIONS) # e.g. llm_aut_m_short-1
com_cols <- paste0("llm_com_m_", CONDITIONS)
rel_cols <- paste0("llm_rel_m_", CONDITIONS)

stopifnot(
  "all LLM condition columns present in wide_rq2" =
    all(c(aut_cols, com_cols, rel_cols) %in% names(wide_rq2))
)

# ── Bootstrap statistic functions ────────────────────────────────────────────

# RQ2a: returns 27 SDs (9 per need). Resampling unit = personas.
# na.rm = TRUE in bootstrap (vs na.rm = FALSE for observed statistic) to avoid
# NA propagation if any persona-condition cell is NA after aggregation.
boot_fn_sd <- function(data, indices) {
  d <- data[indices, ]
  c(
    setNames(
      vapply(aut_cols, function(col) sd(d[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1L)),
      paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)
    ),
    setNames(
      vapply(com_cols, function(col) sd(d[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1L)),
      paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)
    ),
    setNames(
      vapply(rel_cols, function(col) sd(d[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1L)),
      paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)
    )
  )
}

# RQ2b: returns 27 Pearson r values (9 per need). Raw r on bootstrap resamples;
# no Fisher-z in bootstrap — tanh(z1-z2) ≠ r1-r2 (Analytical Strategy §RQ2b).
# Δr = r1-r2 is computed post-hoc from the raw r values.
boot_fn_r <- function(data, indices) {
  d <- data[indices, ]
  c(
    setNames(
      vapply(aut_cols,
             function(col) cor(d[[col]], d$q_aut_mean, use = "complete.obs"),
             numeric(1L)),
      paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)
    ),
    setNames(
      vapply(com_cols,
             function(col) cor(d[[col]], d$q_com_mean, use = "complete.obs"),
             numeric(1L)),
      paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)
    ),
    setNames(
      vapply(rel_cols,
             function(col) cor(d[[col]], d$q_rel_mean, use = "complete.obs"),
             numeric(1L)),
      paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)
    )
  )
}

# RQ2a: returns 27 means (9 per need). Same structure as boot_fn_sd.
# Used for bootstrap CIs for the mean (scores are not normally distributed).
boot_fn_mean <- function(data, indices) {
  d <- data[indices, ]
  c(
    setNames(
      vapply(aut_cols, function(col) mean(d[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1L)),
      paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)
    ),
    setNames(
      vapply(com_cols, function(col) mean(d[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1L)),
      paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)
    ),
    setNames(
      vapply(rel_cols, function(col) mean(d[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1L)),
      paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)
    )
  )
}

# ── Run bootstraps (one call each; all reuse the same wide_rq2) ──────────────
# Convert to base data.frame: [.data.frame is faster than [.tbl_df in tight loops.
wide_rq2_df <- as.data.frame(wide_rq2)
# set.seed here for reproducibility of all bootstrap objects.
set.seed(2026)
boot_sd_rq2   <- boot::boot(data = wide_rq2_df, statistic = boot_fn_sd,   R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
boot_r_rq2    <- boot::boot(data = wide_rq2_df, statistic = boot_fn_r,    R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
boot_mean_rq2 <- boot::boot(data = wide_rq2_df, statistic = boot_fn_mean, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
# boot::boot names $t0 from the statistic return value but leaves $t columns
# unnamed. Assign names so downstream code can index $t by name.
colnames(boot_sd_rq2$t)   <- names(boot_sd_rq2$t0)
colnames(boot_r_rq2$t)    <- names(boot_r_rq2$t0)
colnames(boot_mean_rq2$t) <- names(boot_mean_rq2$t0)

# ── Augment llm_cond_stats with bootstrap CIs for SD ─────────────────────────
# llm_cond_stats$ci_lo/ci_hi are CIs for the *mean*. These add percentile CIs
# for the SD, extracted from boot_sd_rq2.
boot_ci_sd <- purrr::map_dfr(
  list(
    list(prefix = "aut", label = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    list(prefix = "com", label = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    list(prefix = "rel", label = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  ),
  function(x) {
    col_idx <- paste0(x$prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
    boot_t  <- boot_sd_rq2$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]
    tibble::tibble(
      condition  = CONDITIONS,
      subscale   = x$label,
      ci_lo_boot = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_hi_boot = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  }
)
llm_cond_stats <- llm_cond_stats |>
  dplyr::left_join(boot_ci_sd, by = c("condition", "subscale"))

# ── Bootstrap CIs for mean ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
boot_ci_mean <- purrr::map_dfr(
  list(
    list(prefix = "aut", label = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    list(prefix = "com", label = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    list(prefix = "rel", label = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  ),
  function(x) {
    col_idx <- paste0(x$prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
    boot_t  <- boot_mean_rq2$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]
    tibble::tibble(
      condition     = CONDITIONS,
      subscale      = x$label,
      ci_lo_m_boot  = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_hi_m_boot  = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  }
)
llm_cond_stats <- llm_cond_stats |>
  dplyr::left_join(boot_ci_mean, by = c("condition", "subscale"))

# ── Bootstrap CIs for r (wide: one row per condition) ────────────────────────
# Used by tbl_rq2b_obs. Wide format mirrors the table structure (r per need as
# separate columns).
boot_ci_r <- tibble::tibble(
  condition = CONDITIONS,
  ci_lo_aut = apply(boot_r_rq2$t[, paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS), drop = FALSE], 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
  ci_hi_aut = apply(boot_r_rq2$t[, paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS), drop = FALSE], 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE),
  ci_lo_com = apply(boot_r_rq2$t[, paste0("com_", CONDITIONS), drop = FALSE], 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
  ci_hi_com = apply(boot_r_rq2$t[, paste0("com_", CONDITIONS), drop = FALSE], 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE),
  ci_lo_rel = apply(boot_r_rq2$t[, paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS), drop = FALSE], 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
  ci_hi_rel = apply(boot_r_rq2$t[, paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS), drop = FALSE], 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
)

# ── Shared formatting helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

# 7 pre-specified endpoint contrasts (Analytical Strategy §RQ2a / §RQ2b).
# Across-length contrasts (SD300-SD50 / r300-r50) use fully independent texts.
# Across-n_texts contrasts reflect cumulative structure — CIs are correlated
# observations; this must be stated explicitly when reporting.
compute_contrasts_rq2 <- function(vals) {
  # vals: named vector with CONDITIONS as names ("short-1", "long-5", etc.)
  c(
    "Length (long-short) | 1 text"        = vals[["long-1"]]   - vals[["short-1"]],
    "Length (long-short) | 3 texts"       = vals[["long-3"]]   - vals[["short-3"]],
    "Length (long-short) | 5 texts"       = vals[["long-5"]]   - vals[["short-5"]],
    "Texts (5-1) | short"                 = vals[["short-5"]]  - vals[["short-1"]],
    "Texts (5-1) | medium"                = vals[["medium-5"]] - vals[["medium-1"]],
    "Texts (5-1) | long"                  = vals[["long-5"]]   - vals[["long-1"]],
    "Grand diagonal (long-5 - short-1)"   = vals[["long-5"]]   - vals[["short-1"]]
  )
}

# 3x3 matrix of stat [95% bootstrap CI] strings. Works for both SD (RQ2a)
# and Pearson r (RQ2b).
format_rq2_matrix <- function(boot_obj, need_prefix) {
  col_idx <- paste0(need_prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
  t0      <- boot_obj$t0[col_idx]
  boot_t  <- boot_obj$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]
  ci_l    <- apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE)
  ci_u    <- apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)

  tibble::tibble(
    condition = CONDITIONS,
    value     = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", t0, ci_l, ci_u)
  ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
    dplyr::mutate(text_count = factor(text_count, levels = N_TEXTS_VARIANTS)) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = text_count, values_from = value) |>
    dplyr::select(-condition) |>
    dplyr::arrange(target_words) |>
    dplyr::mutate(target_words = WORD_LENGTH_LABELS[as.character(target_words)])
}

# 7-row contrast table with 95% bootstrap CIs.
format_rq2_contrasts <- function(boot_obj, need_prefix, delta_label = "Estimate") {
  col_idx   <- paste0(need_prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
  t0        <- setNames(boot_obj$t0[col_idx], CONDITIONS)
  boot_t    <- boot_obj$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]

  delta_t0   <- compute_contrasts_rq2(t0)
  delta_boot <- t(apply(boot_t, 1L, function(row) {
    names(row) <- CONDITIONS
    compute_contrasts_rq2(row)
  }))

  ci_l <- apply(delta_boot, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE)
  ci_u <- apply(delta_boot, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)

  tibble::tibble(
    Contrast        = names(delta_t0),
    !!delta_label  := sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", delta_t0, ci_l, ci_u)
  )
}

# 7-row contrast table with estimate, SE, 95% CI, and Wald p-value (separate cols).
format_rq2_contrasts_full <- function(boot_obj, need_prefix) {
  col_idx    <- paste0(need_prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
  t0         <- setNames(boot_obj$t0[col_idx], CONDITIONS)
  boot_t     <- boot_obj$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]

  delta_t0   <- compute_contrasts_rq2(t0)
  delta_boot <- t(apply(boot_t, 1L, function(row) {
    names(row) <- CONDITIONS
    compute_contrasts_rq2(row)
  }))

  se   <- apply(delta_boot, 2L, sd,       na.rm = TRUE)
  ci_l <- apply(delta_boot, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE)
  ci_u <- apply(delta_boot, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
  p    <- 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(delta_t0 / se)))

  tibble::tibble(
    Contrast = names(delta_t0),
    Estimate = round(delta_t0, 2L),
    SE       = round(se,       2L),
    LL       = round(ci_l,     2L),
    UL       = round(ci_u,     2L),
    p        = ifelse(p < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", p))
  )
}

# Line plot for RQ2a (SD) or RQ2b (r). x = target_words, lines = text_count.
# ref_line: optional horizontal reference line (e.g. 0 for r, NULL for SD).
plot_rq2_lines <- function(boot_obj, need_prefix, y_label, plot_title,
                            ref_line = NULL) {
  col_idx    <- paste0(need_prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
  boot_t     <- boot_obj$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]

  plot_data <- tibble::tibble(
    condition = CONDITIONS,
    value     = boot_obj$t0[col_idx],
    ci_lo     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
    ci_hi     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
    dplyr::mutate(text_count = factor(text_count, levels = N_TEXTS_VARIANTS))

  p <- ggplot(plot_data,
              aes(x = target_words, y = value,
                  group = text_count, color = text_count)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 10, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
      labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_TEXTS) +
    labs(
      title    = plot_title,
      subtitle = paste0("N = ", nrow(wide_rq2),
                        " AI surrogates; error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = ",
                        BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ")"),
      x     = "Target word length",
      y     = y_label,
      color = "Texts (n)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
    theme(
      text            = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )

  if (!is.null(ref_line)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = ref_line, linetype = "dashed",
                        color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5)
  }
  p
}
```

## 7.4 Assumption Checks for Pearson *r* (RQ2b)

### 7.4.1 Linearity

The scatter plots below show LLM score (x) against questionnaire subscale score (y) for each of the 27 condition × need combinations. The LOESS smoother (solid) and OLS fit (dashed) are overlaid; close agreement between the two lines supports the linearity assumption required for Pearson *r*.

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-assump-scatter
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: !expr paste0("Scatter plots of LLM score (x-axis) vs. questionnaire subscale score (y-axis) for all 9 conditions × 3 need dimensions (N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates per panel). Points are jittered to reveal density at integer-valued scores. Solid line = LOESS smoother; dashed line = OLS regression fit.")
#| fig-width: 20
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-dpi: 150

# r labels from observed bootstrap statistics
r_labels_scatter <- tibble::tibble(
  condition = rep(CONDITIONS, times = 3),
  need      = rep(c("aut", "com", "rel"), each = length(CONDITIONS)),
  r_val     = c(
    boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)],
    boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)],
    boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)]
  )
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need_label = factor(
      dplyr::case_when(
        need == "aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
        need == "com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
        need == "rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
      ),
      levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)
    ),
    condition = factor(condition, levels = CONDITIONS),
    label     = paste0("r = ", sprintf("%.2f", r_val)),
    x_pos     = 1.2,
    y_pos     = 4.85
  )

# Long-form data: one row per persona × condition × need
long_rq2_scatter <- wide_rq2 |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols          = dplyr::matches("^llm_(aut|com|rel)_m_"),
    names_to      = c("need", "condition"),
    names_pattern = "llm_(aut|com|rel)_m_(.*)"
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(llm_score = value) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    q_score = dplyr::case_when(
      need == "aut" ~ q_aut_mean,
      need == "com" ~ q_com_mean,
      need == "rel" ~ q_rel_mean
    ),
    need_label = factor(
      dplyr::case_when(
        need == "aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
        need == "com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
        need == "rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
      ),
      levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)
    ),
    condition = factor(condition, levels = CONDITIONS)
  )

fig_rq2b_assump_scatter <- ggplot(long_rq2_scatter, aes(x = llm_score, y = q_score)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2, width = 0.15, height = 0.15, stroke = 0,
              colour = COL_AI_SINGLE) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", colour = "steelblue", se = FALSE,
              linewidth = 0.7, formula = y ~ x,
              method.args = list(span = 1)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", colour = "firebrick", linetype = "dashed",
              se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.7, formula = y ~ x) +
  geom_text(
    data        = r_labels_scatter,
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = label),
    hjust       = 0, size = 2.5, colour = "grey30", inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_grid(need_label ~ condition) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 5.2), breaks = c(1, 3, 5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 5.2), breaks = c(1, 3, 5)) +
  labs(x = "LLM score", y = "Questionnaire score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    text             = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 8)
  )

fig_rq2b_assump_scatter
```

# 8 RQ2: LLM Textual Conditions

RQ2 examines how the quantity of textual information affects the LLM's discriminating power (RQ2a) and its correspondence with questionnaire scores (RQ2b). This section moves from validation of the measurement to exploring the limits of the LLM's inferential capabilities across the 3×3 factorial design.

## 8.1 RQ2a: Between-Surrogate Variability (SD)

### 8.1.1 Table with Bootstrap Confidence Intervals

The table below presents the between-surrogate SD of surrogate-level mean LLM scores for each condition. Following your strategy, we use bootstrap resamples to generate CIs

```{r}
#| label: tbl_rq2a_display
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Between-Surrogate SD of LLM Scores per Experimental Condition (RQ2a)"

# 1. Prepare Table Data --------------------------------------------------------
tbl_rq2a_display <- llm_cond_stats |>
  mutate(Result = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", sd, ci_lo_boot, ci_hi_boot)) |>
  dplyr::select(condition, subscale, Result) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = subscale, values_from = Result) |>
  mutate(condition = factor(condition, levels = CONDITIONS)) |>
  arrange(condition)

# 2. Render APA Table  ------------------------------------------------
tbl_rq2a_display |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    condition = "Experimental Condition"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "LLM Score SD per Dimension"),
    colwidths = c(1, 3)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(
    paste0("N = ", n_distinct(d_conditions$uuid), " AI surrogates. ",
           "Values = SD [95% bootstrap percentile CI]. ",
           "SD = between-surrogate variability of surrogate-level mean LLM scores. ",
           "R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ".")
  ) |>
  autofit()
```





```{r}
#| label: helper-rq2a-bootstrap
#| include: true

# 1. Helper: Calculate SD and Bootstrap CI for a specific condition/need -------
# This function isolates the RQ2a requirement: the uncertainty of the SD estimate.
calc_rq2a_sd_boot <- function(data, target_need, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES) {
  
  # Extract surrogate-level means for the specific need and condition
  scores <- data[[target_need]]
  
  # Observed SD (between-surrogate)
  sd_obs <- sd(scores, na.rm = FALSE) # Strict Grid: propagate NAs
  
  if (is.na(sd_obs) || length(na.omit(scores)) < 2) return(NA)
  
  # Bootstrap the SD estimate
  boot_res <- boot::boot(
    data = scores, 
    statistic = function(x, i) sd(x[i], na.rm = TRUE), 
    R = R
  )
  
  # Percentile CIs 
  ci_res <- boot::boot.ci(boot_res, type = "perc")
  
  return(list(
    sd    = sd_obs,
    ci_lo = ci_res$percent[4],
    ci_hi = ci_res$percent[5]
  ))
}
```




```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2a-matrices
#| include: false
#| echo: false

#NOT USED
### 8.1.1 SD Matrix per Psychological Need (LLM Scores)


# 1. Helper Function to Generate a 3x3 Matrix for a Specific Need -------------
# BUG (fixed): the old version used llm_cond_stats$ci_lo / ci_hi which are
# CIs for the *mean*, not for the SD. Now delegates to format_rq2_matrix()
# which extracts bootstrap percentile CIs for the SD from boot_sd_rq2.
render_sd_matrix <- function(target_need_label) {
  need_prefix <- switch(target_need_label,
    "Autonomy"    = "aut",
    "Competence"  = "com",
    "Relatedness" = "rel"
  )

  format_rq2_matrix(boot_sd_rq2, need_prefix) |>
    flextable() |>
    style_apa7() |>
    set_header_labels(target_words = "Length") |>
    add_header_row(
      values    = c("", "Number of Texts (n)"),
      colwidths = c(1L, 3L)
    ) |>
    set_caption(paste("Table: Between-Surrogate SD [95% Bootstrap CI] —",
                      target_need_label)) |>
    add_apa_note(
      paste0("N = ", n_distinct(d_conditions$uuid), " AI surrogates. ",
             "Values represent SD [95% bootstrap percentile CI] of surrogate-level means. ",
             "R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ".")
    ) |>
    autofit()
}

# 2. Sequential Rendering for each Need  ------------------------------
# We iterate through the NEEDS constant to produce three separate tables
for (need in NEEDS) {
  print(render_sd_matrix(need))
}
```


### 8.1.2 Line charts for Between Surrogate SD

The plot visualizes whether discriminating power changes with information quantity. Text length (x-axis) uses fully independent observations; n_texts levels are cumulative within each length block (stated in error-bar interpretation).

#### Per Need

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-sd-lines-new
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Between-surrogate SD of surrogate-level mean LLM scores by condition (RQ2a). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5); x-axis = target word length (fully independent). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_rq2a_data <- purrr::map_dfr(
  list(
    list(prefix = "aut", label = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    list(prefix = "com", label = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    list(prefix = "rel", label = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  ),
  function(x) {
    col_idx <- paste0(x$prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
    boot_t  <- boot_sd_rq2$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]
    tibble::tibble(
      condition = CONDITIONS,
      need      = x$label,
      sd        = boot_sd_rq2$t0[col_idx],
      ci_lo     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_hi     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) |>
      dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
      dplyr::mutate(text_count = factor(text_count, levels = N_TEXTS_VARIANTS))
  }
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
                                               TABLE_LABEL_COM,
                                               TABLE_LABEL_REL)))


fig_rq2a_sd_lines <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = sd,
           group = text_count, color = text_count)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, alpha = 0.7, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_TEXTS) +
  labs(
    title    = "RQ2a: Between-Surrogate SD by Condition",
    subtitle = paste0("N = ", nrow(wide_rq2),
                      " AI surrogates; error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = ",
                      BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ")"),
    x     = "Target word length",
    y     = "Between-surrogate SD",
    color = "Texts (n)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
  )

fig_rq2a_sd_lines
```

#### Shaded

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-sd-lines-shaded
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Between-surrogate SD of surrogate-level mean LLM scores by condition (RQ2a). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5), shaded light to dark; x-axis = target word length (fully independent). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

fig_rq2a_sd_lines_shaded <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = sd,
           group = text_count, color = text_count, fill = text_count)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.12, color = NA, position = DODGE) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.2, linetype = "solid", position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Target word length",
    y = "Between-surrogate SD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.18, 0.2),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq2a_sd_lines_shaded
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). Line shading light to dark = 1, 3, 5 texts.

### 8.1.4b OLS Trend in Condition SD

Simple OLS regression of condition-level SD on text length and text count (both continuous, centred at the design midpoint: 150 words, 3 texts). N = 9 conditions per need; 3 parameters → 6 df residual. SEs are expected to be large.

```{r}
#| label: ols-sd-fit
#| include: false

sd_reg_data <- fig_rq2a_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    words_c  = as.numeric(target_words) - 150L,
    ntexts_c = as.numeric(as.character(text_count)) - 3L
  )

extract_lm_sd <- function(label) {
  m <- lm(sd ~ words_c + ntexts_c,
           data = dplyr::filter(sd_reg_data, need == label))
  s <- summary(m)$coefficients
  tibble::tibble(
    need     = label,
    term     = rownames(s),
    estimate = s[, "Estimate"],
    se       = s[, "Std. Error"],
    t        = s[, "t value"],
    p        = s[, "Pr(>|t|)"]
  )
}

lm_sd_coefs <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  extract_lm_sd(TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
  extract_lm_sd(TABLE_LABEL_COM),
  extract_lm_sd(TABLE_LABEL_REL)
)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-sd-trend
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Between-surrogate SD by condition with OLS trend line (RQ2a, internal). Dashed line = pooled OLS fit across all 9 conditions within each need panel (sd ~ target_words, marginal). "
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

#DODGE <- position_dodge(width = 18) # distance between points 


ggplot(fig_rq2a_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = sd,
           group = text_count, color = text_count, fill = text_count)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.1, linetype = "solid", position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(x = target_words, y = sd, group = 1),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    method      = "lm",
    se          = FALSE,
    color       = COL_HU_SHADES[4],
    #COL_HU_SHADES = c("#BE2A16", "#CE5545", "#DC7D71", "#E9A39B", "#F5C9C5","#FFFFFF")
    linetype    = "dashed",
    linewidth   = 1.0
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_TEXTS, name = "Number of texts") +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = COL_AI_TEXTS, name = "Number of texts") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.25, 0.55)) +
  labs(
    x = "Target word length",
    y = "Between-surrogate SD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position    = c(0.18, 0.2),
    legend.direction   = "horizontal",
    legend.margin      = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key         = element_blank(),
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "plain")
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-sd-trend-wide
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Between-surrogate SD by condition with OLS trend line (RQ2a, internal). Dashed line = pooled OLS fit across all 9 conditions within each need panel (sd ~ target_words, marginal). Y-axis expanded to 0–2."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400



ggplot(fig_rq2a_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = sd,
           group = text_count, color = text_count, fill = text_count)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.1, linetype = "solid", position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(x = target_words, y = sd, group = 1),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    method      = "lm",
    se          = FALSE,
    color       = COL_HU_SHADES[4],
    linetype    = "dashed",
    linewidth   = 1.0
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = max(fig_rq2a_data$ci_hi), color = "grey60", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = min(fig_rq2a_data$ci_lo), color = "grey60", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_TEXTS, name = "Number of texts") +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = COL_AI_TEXTS, name = "Number of texts") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.75)) +
  labs(
    x = "Target word length",
    y = "Between-surrogate SD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position    = c(0.18, 0.2),
    legend.direction   = "horizontal",
    legend.margin      = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key         = element_blank(),
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "plain")
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2a-sd-ols
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "OLS Regression of Condition SD on Text Length and Text Count per Need (internal — not reported)"

term_labels_sd <- c(
  "(Intercept)" = "Intercept (SD at 150 words, 3 texts)",
  "words_c"     = "Word length (per word, centred at 150)",
  "ntexts_c"    = "Text count (per text, centred at 3)"
)

lm_sd_coefs |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    term = term_labels_sd[term]
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = term,
    names_from  = need,
    values_from = c(estimate, se, t, p),
    names_glue  = "{need}_{.value}"
  ) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    Term = term,
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, " b")  := sprintf("%.4f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_estimate")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, " SE") := sprintf("%.4f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_se")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, " p")  := ifelse(.data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_p")]] < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_p")]])),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, " b")  := sprintf("%.4f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_estimate")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, " SE") := sprintf("%.4f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_se")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, " p")  := ifelse(.data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_p")]] < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_p")]])),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, " b")  := sprintf("%.4f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_estimate")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, " SE") := sprintf("%.4f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_se")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, " p")  := ifelse(.data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_p")]] < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_p")]]))
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1L, 3L, 3L, 3L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1L, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "N = 9 conditions per need (3 lengths x 3 text counts); 6 df residual. ",
    "b = OLS coefficient. SE = standard error. ",
    "Predictors centred at 150 words and 3 texts. "
  )) |>
  autofit()
```


#### Shaded, texts on x-axis

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-sd-lines-shaded-by-texts
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Between-surrogate SD of surrogate-level mean LLM scores by condition (RQ2a). Lines = target word length (50, 150, 300), shaded light to dark; x-axis = number of texts (fully independent). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

fig_rq2a_sd_lines_shaded_by_texts <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_data,
       aes(x = text_count, y = sd,
           group = factor(target_words), color = factor(target_words), fill = factor(target_words))) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.12, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.2, linetype = "solid") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_discrete() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = COL_AI_WORDS,
    name   = "Target word length"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = COL_AI_WORDS,
    name   = "Target word length"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of texts",
    y = "Between-surrogate SD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.18, 0.2),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq2a_sd_lines_shaded_by_texts
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). Line shading light to dark = 50, 150, 300 target words.

#### Shaded, actual word count on x-axis

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-sd-lines-shaded-by-actual-words
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Between-surrogate SD of surrogate-level mean LLM scores by condition (RQ2a). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5), shaded light to dark; x-axis = mean actual word count per condition. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

mean_actual_words <- d |>
  dplyr::group_by(condition) |>
  dplyr::summarise(mean_actual_words = mean(actual_words, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

fig_rq2a_sd_actual_data <- fig_rq2a_data |>
  dplyr::left_join(mean_actual_words, by = "condition")

fig_rq2a_sd_lines_shaded_by_actual_words <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_sd_actual_data,
       aes(x = mean_actual_words, y = sd,
           group = text_count, color = text_count, fill = text_count)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.12, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.2, linetype = "solid") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1500),
    breaks = c(0, 50, 150, 250, 400, 750, 1250, 1500)
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Mean actual word count",
    y = "Between-surrogate SD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.18, 0.2),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq2a_sd_lines_shaded_by_actual_words
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). x-axis shows mean actual word count per condition; line shading light to dark = 1, 3, 5 texts.

#### By volume

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-sd-by-volume
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Between-surrogate SD of surrogate-level mean LLM scores by total word volume (RQ2a). Lines = need dimension; x-axis = target words × text count; each point = one condition. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_rq2a_vol_data <- fig_rq2a_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(total_volume = as.integer(as.character(target_words)) * as.integer(as.character(text_count))) |>
  dplyr::arrange(need, total_volume)

pd_vol <- position_dodge(width = 30)

fig_rq2a_sd_by_volume <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_vol_data,
       aes(x = total_volume, y = sd, group = need, color = need)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, position = pd_vol) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, position = pd_vol) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, alpha = 0.7, position = pd_vol) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(fig_rq2a_vol_data$total_volume)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_LLM) +
  labs(
    title    = "RQ2a: Between-Surrogate SD by Total Word Volume",
    subtitle = paste0("N = ", nrow(wide_rq2),
                      " AI surrogates; error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = ",
                      BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ")"),
    x     = "Total word volume (target words × text count)",
    y     = "Between-surrogate SD",
    color = "Need"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text            = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

fig_rq2a_sd_by_volume
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-sd-by-volume-bubble
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Between-surrogate SD of surrogate-level mean LLM scores by total word volume (RQ2a). Lines = need dimension; x-axis = target words × text count; bubble size = number of texts (1, 3, 5). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_rq2a_sd_by_volume_bubble <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_vol_data,
       aes(x = total_volume, y = sd, group = need, color = need,
           size = as.numeric(as.character(text_count)))) +
  geom_line(aes(size = NULL), linewidth = 0.4, position = pd_vol, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(position = pd_vol, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi, size = NULL), width = 0, alpha = 0.7,
                linewidth = 0.5, position = pd_vol) +
```
