```{r}
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(fig_rq2a_vol_data$total_volume)) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 7), breaks = c(1, 3, 5), name = "Text count") +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_LLM) +
  labs(
    x       = "Total word volume (target words × text count)",
    y       = "Between-surrogate SD",
    color   = "Need",
    caption = paste0("Note. N = ", nrow(wide_rq2),
                     " AI surrogates. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = ",
                     BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). Bubble size = number of texts (1, 3, 5).")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    axis.text.x        = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.caption       = element_text(face = "italic", hjust = 0, size = rel(0.85))
  )

fig_rq2a_sd_by_volume_bubble
```

### 8.1.3 Line charts for  Means

Mean LLM score per condition (surrogate-level means, collapsed across 3 repeats). Shown alongside the SD charts in 8.1.2 to read signal pattern and variability in parallel.

#### Per Need

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-mean-lines
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Mean LLM score (surrogate-level) by condition (RQ2a). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5); x-axis = target word length (fully independent). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_rq2a_mean_data <- purrr::map_dfr(
  list(
    list(prefix = "aut", label = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    list(prefix = "com", label = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    list(prefix = "rel", label = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  ),
  function(x) {
    col_idx <- paste0(x$prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
    boot_t  <- boot_mean_rq2$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]
    tibble::tibble(
      condition = CONDITIONS,
      need      = x$label,
      mean      = boot_mean_rq2$t0[col_idx],
      ci_lo     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_hi     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) |>
      dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
      dplyr::mutate(text_count = factor(text_count, levels = N_TEXTS_VARIANTS))
  }
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
                                               TABLE_LABEL_COM,
                                               TABLE_LABEL_REL)))


fig_rq2a_mean_lines <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_mean_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = mean,
           group = text_count, color = text_count)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, alpha = 0.7, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 5)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_TEXTS) +
  labs(
    title    = "RQ2a: Mean LLM Score by Condition",
    subtitle = paste0("N = ", nrow(wide_rq2),
                      " AI surrogates; error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = ",
                      BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ")"),
    x     = "Target word length",
    y     = "Mean LLM score",
    color = "Texts (n)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text            = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)
  )

fig_rq2a_mean_lines
```

#### Shaded

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-mean-lines-shaded
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: "Mean LLM score (surrogate-level) by condition (RQ2a). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5), shaded light to dark; x-axis = target word length (fully independent). Red dashed line = OLS regression of mean score on word length (marginalised over text count)."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_rq2a_mean_lines_shaded <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_mean_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = mean,
           group = text_count, color = text_count)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4, linetype = "solid", position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 5)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Target word length",
    y = "Mean LLM score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text                 = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y   = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y   = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    legend.position      = c(0.1, 0.2),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.justification = c("left", "bottom"),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
    

  
    
  )

fig_rq2a_mean_lines_shaded
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Line shading light to dark = 1, 3, 5 texts.

#### By volume

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-mean-by-volume-shaded
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Mean LLM score (surrogate-level) by total word volume (RQ2a). Lines = need dimension; x-axis = target words × text count; ribbon = 95% bootstrap CI band. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_rq2a_mean_vol_data <- fig_rq2a_mean_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(total_volume = as.integer(as.character(target_words)) * as.integer(as.character(text_count))) |>
  dplyr::arrange(need, total_volume)

pd_mean_vol <- position_dodge(width = 30)

fig_rq2a_mean_by_volume_shaded <- ggplot(fig_rq2a_mean_vol_data,
       aes(x = total_volume, y = mean, group = need, color = need, fill = need)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.12, color = NA, position = pd_mean_vol) +
  geom_line(aes(fill = NULL), linewidth = 0.4, linetype = "solid", position = pd_mean_vol) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi, fill = NULL), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8, position = pd_mean_vol) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8, position = pd_mean_vol) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(fig_rq2a_mean_vol_data$total_volume)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 5)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_LLM, name = "Need") +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = COL_AI_LLM, name = "Need") +
  labs(
    x = "Total word volume (target words × text count)",
    y = "Mean LLM score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text                 = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    axis.text.x          = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    panel.grid.major.x   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y   = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y   = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    legend.position      = c(0.02, 0.08),
    legend.justification = c("left", "bottom"),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "italic")
  )

fig_rq2a_mean_by_volume_shaded
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`).

This analysis assesses the LLM's "discriminating power"—its ability to spread AI Surrogates apart based on the text provided. A near-zero SD would indicate a scoring collapse, where the LLM assigns similar scores regardless of the persona's underlying traits.

### 8.1.4 Bootstrap Contrasts

7 pre-specified endpoint contrasts per need (ΔSD). Across-length contrasts use fully
independent texts (strongest evidence). Across-n_texts contrasts reflect
cumulative text structure; CIs reflect correlated observations.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2a-contrasts
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Bootstrap Contrasts ΔSD [95% CI] per Need"

format_rq2_contrasts(boot_sd_rq2, "aut", delta_label = TABLE_LABEL_AUT) |>
  dplyr::rename(!!TABLE_LABEL_AUT := 2) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    format_rq2_contrasts(boot_sd_rq2, "com", delta_label = TABLE_LABEL_COM) |>
      dplyr::rename(!!TABLE_LABEL_COM := 2),
    by = "Contrast"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    format_rq2_contrasts(boot_sd_rq2, "rel", delta_label = TABLE_LABEL_REL) |>
      dplyr::rename(!!TABLE_LABEL_REL := 2),
    by = "Contrast"
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "ΔSD [95% Bootstrap CI]"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 3L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "ΔSD = SD₁ − SD₂. Bootstrap percentile CIs, R = ",
    BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ". ",
    "Across-n_texts contrasts reflect cumulative text structure; CIs are correlated observations."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```


```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2a-contrasts-full
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Bootstrap Contrasts ΔSD with SE and p-Values per Need"

build_contrast_need_sd <- function(prefix) {
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_sd_rq2, prefix) |>
    dplyr::rename_with(~ paste0(., "_", prefix), -Contrast)
}

build_contrast_need_sd("aut") |>
  dplyr::left_join(build_contrast_need_sd("com"), by = "Contrast") |>
  dplyr::left_join(build_contrast_need_sd("rel"), by = "Contrast") |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    Contrast     = "Contrast",
    Estimate_aut = "Estimate", SE_aut = "SE", LL_aut = "LL", UL_aut = "UL", p_aut = "p",
    Estimate_com = "Estimate", SE_com = "SE", LL_com = "LL", UL_com = "UL", p_com = "p",
    Estimate_rel = "Estimate", SE_rel = "SE", LL_rel = "LL", UL_rel = "UL", p_rel = "p"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p",
                      "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p",
                      "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L)
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1L, 5L, 5L, 5L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1:2, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "ΔSD = SD₁ − SD₂. SE = bootstrap SD of the contrast distribution. ",
    "p = Wald test (2×Φ(−|ΔSD/SE|)). ",
    "95% CI = bootstrap percentile interval, R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ". ",
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates. ",
    "Across-n_texts contrasts reflect cumulative text structure; CIs are correlated observations."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-contrasts-forest
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: "Bootstrap contrast estimates (ΔSD) with 95% CIs per need dimension (RQ2a). Each row = one pre-specified endpoint contrast; x-axis = ΔSD; lines = 95% bootstrap percentile CI; dashed vertical line = 0. Dotted horizontal lines separate contrast groups (length effects, text-count effects, grand diagonal)."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

rq2a_contrast_plot_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_sd_rq2, "aut") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_sd_rq2, "com") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_sd_rq2, "rel") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need     = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = rev(unique(Contrast)))
  )

ggplot(rq2a_contrast_plot_data,
       aes(x = Estimate, y = Contrast)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(1.5, 4.5), linetype = "dotted", color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = LL, xend = UL, y = Contrast, yend = Contrast), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  labs(
    x = "ΔSD [95% bootstrap CI]",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.2)
  )
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Bootstrap percentile CIs, R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`. Groups (top to bottom): length effects (ΔSD across word length at fixed text count); text-count effects (ΔSD across text count at fixed length); grand diagonal.


### 8.1.5 Linear Mixed-Effects Regression on Condition Means

To formally test whether condition-level means shift with information quantity, a linear mixed-effects model was fit per need dimension with target word length and text count as fixed effects and persona as a random intercept. Predictors are centered at the design midpoint (150 words, 3 texts) and rescaled to interpretable units (per 100 words; per additional text), so the intercept reflects the estimated mean at the central condition (medium length, 3 texts). *p*-values use the normal approximation on the *t*-statistic, which is appropriate given N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` personas.

```{r}
#| label: lmer-rq2a-fit
#| include: false

d_lmer <- d_conditions |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    words_c = (as.integer(target_words) - 150L) / 100,
    texts_c = as.integer(as.character(text_count)) - 3L
  )

lmer_aut <- lme4::lmer(llm_aut_m ~ words_c + texts_c + (1 | uuid), data = d_lmer, REML = TRUE)
lmer_com <- lme4::lmer(llm_com_m ~ words_c + texts_c + (1 | uuid), data = d_lmer, REML = TRUE)
lmer_rel <- lme4::lmer(llm_rel_m ~ words_c + texts_c + (1 | uuid), data = d_lmer, REML = TRUE)

extract_lmer_fe <- function(model, need_label) {
  coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients
  tibble::tibble(
    need     = need_label,
    term     = rownames(coefs),
    estimate = coefs[, "Estimate"],
    se       = coefs[, "Std. Error"],
    t        = coefs[, "t value"],
    ll       = estimate - qnorm(0.975) * se,
    ul       = estimate + qnorm(0.975) * se,
    p        = 2 * pnorm(-abs(t))
  )
}

lmer_coefs <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  extract_lmer_fe(lmer_aut, TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
  extract_lmer_fe(lmer_com, TABLE_LABEL_COM),
  extract_lmer_fe(lmer_rel, TABLE_LABEL_REL)
) |>
  dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)")
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2a-lmer
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Fixed Effects of Linear Mixed-Effects Models Predicting Condition-Level Mean LLM Score per Need (RQ2a)"

term_labels <- c(
  "words_c" = "Word length (per 100 words, centred at 150)",
  "texts_c" = "Text count (per text, centred at 3)"
)

lmer_coefs |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    term = term_labels[term],
    need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL))
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    id_cols  = term,
    names_from = need,
    values_from = c(estimate, se, ll, ul, p),
    names_glue = "{need}_{.value}"
  ) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    Term = term,
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, " b")  := sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_estimate")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, " SE") := sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_se")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, " CI") := sprintf("[%.3f, %.3f]", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_ll")]], .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_ul")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, " p")  := ifelse(.data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_p")]] < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, "_p")]])),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, " b")  := sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_estimate")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, " SE") := sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_se")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, " CI") := sprintf("[%.3f, %.3f]", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_ll")]], .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_ul")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, " p")  := ifelse(.data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_p")]] < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_COM, "_p")]])),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, " b")  := sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_estimate")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, " SE") := sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_se")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, " CI") := sprintf("[%.3f, %.3f]", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_ll")]], .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_ul")]]),
    !!paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, " p")  := ifelse(.data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_p")]] < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", .data[[paste0(TABLE_LABEL_REL, "_p")]]))
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1L, 4L, 4L, 4L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1L, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "b = unstandardized regression coefficient. SE = standard error. ",
    "95% CI = Wald interval. p = two-sided z-test (normal approximation on t). ",
    "Predictors centred at 150 words and 3 texts respectively. ",
    "Random intercept per persona (uuid). N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " personas, ",
    nrow(d_lmer), " observations."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-lmer-forest
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: "Fixed-effect coefficients from linear mixed-effects models predicting condition-level mean LLM score (RQ2a). Each panel = one need dimension; rows = predictors; error bars = 95% Wald CIs; dashed line = 0."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 3
#| fig-dpi: 300

lmer_plot_data <- lmer_coefs |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    term = dplyr::recode(term,
      "words_c" = "Word length\n(per 100 words)",
      "texts_c" = "Text count\n(per text)"
    ),
    term = factor(term, levels = rev(unique(term)))
  )

ggplot(lmer_plot_data, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = ll, xend = ul, y = term, yend = term), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  labs(x = "b [95% Wald CI]", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.2)
  )
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` personas. Random intercept per persona; predictors centred and rescaled. *p*-values use normal approximation on *t*.


### 8.1.6 Bootstrap ΔMean Contrasts

Same 7 endpoint contrasts applied to the condition-level means (ΔMean = Mean₁ − Mean₂). A systematic shift in means with information quantity would indicate scoring-level bias or a consistent contextual anchor effect, distinct from the spread question in §8.1.4.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2a-mean-contrasts-full
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Bootstrap Contrasts ΔMean with SE and p-Values per Need (RQ2a)"

build_contrast_need_mean <- function(prefix) {
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_mean_rq2, prefix) |>
    dplyr::rename_with(~ paste0(., "_", prefix), -Contrast)
}

build_contrast_need_mean("aut") |>
  dplyr::left_join(build_contrast_need_mean("com"), by = "Contrast") |>
  dplyr::left_join(build_contrast_need_mean("rel"), by = "Contrast") |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    Contrast     = "Contrast",
    Estimate_aut = "Estimate", SE_aut = "SE", LL_aut = "LL", UL_aut = "UL", p_aut = "p",
    Estimate_com = "Estimate", SE_com = "SE", LL_com = "LL", UL_com = "UL", p_com = "p",
    Estimate_rel = "Estimate", SE_rel = "SE", LL_rel = "LL", UL_rel = "UL", p_rel = "p"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p",
                      "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p",
                      "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L)
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1L, 5L, 5L, 5L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1:2, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "ΔMean = Mean₁ − Mean₂ of surrogate-level mean LLM scores. ",
    "SE = bootstrap SD. p = Wald test (2×Φ(−|ΔMean/SE|)). ",
    "95% CI = bootstrap percentile interval, R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ". ",
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates. ",
    "Across-n_texts contrasts reflect cumulative text structure; CIs are correlated observations."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2a-mean-contrasts-forest
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Bootstrap contrast estimates (ΔMean) with 95% CIs per need dimension (RQ2a). Each row = one pre-specified endpoint contrast; x-axis = ΔMean; lines = 95% bootstrap percentile CI; dashed vertical line = 0. Dotted horizontal lines separate contrast groups."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

rq2a_mean_contrast_plot_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_mean_rq2, "aut") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_mean_rq2, "com") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_mean_rq2, "rel") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need     = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = rev(unique(Contrast)))
  )

ggplot(rq2a_mean_contrast_plot_data,
       aes(x = Estimate, y = Contrast)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(1.5, 4.5), linetype = "dotted", color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = LL, xend = UL, y = Contrast, yend = Contrast), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  labs(
    x = "ΔMean [95% bootstrap CI]",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.2)
  )
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Bootstrap percentile CIs, R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`. Groups (top to bottom): length effects (ΔMean across word length at fixed text count); text-count effects (ΔMean across text count at fixed length); grand diagonal.


## 8.2 RQ2b: LLM–Questionnaire Correspondence

RQ2b examines whether between-surrogate variability (RQ2a) corresponds to questionnaire scores. For each of the 9 conditions, Pearson *r* is computed between surrogate-level mean LLM scores (mean of 3 scoring calls) and questionnaire subscale scores, separately per need dimension. This yields a 3×3 matrix of *r* values per need.

*Assumption check.* Pearson *r* is used throughout (stronger inference than Spearman; Analytical Strategy §RQ2b). Spearman's ρ is computed as a robustness check in the internal chunk below.

*Circularity caveat.* A monotonic increase in *r* with information quantity is
consistent with increasing scorer sensitivity but equally consistent with the
LLM scorer extracting the same surface-level linguistic features that drove the
surrogate's questionnaire responses from the same persona description. These two
explanations cannot be distinguished from RQ2b alone.

*Interpretive prior* (Peters & Matz, 2024; Wright et al., 2026). *r* is
expected to stabilise at ~.30 around 1,400–1,500 words. The 300w × 5-text
condition (~1,500 words total) should yield the strongest correlations; the
50w × 1-text condition the weakest. The grand diagonal contrast
Δ*r* = *r*(300w×5t) − *r*(50w×1t) maps directly onto this prediction.

### 8.2.1 Table Pearson Correlations r 


```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-observed-r
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Observed Pearson r (LLM score vs. questionnaire subscale score) per Condition and Need"

tbl_rq2b_obs <- tibble::tibble(
  condition = CONDITIONS,
  r_aut     = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)],
  r_com     = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)],
  r_rel     = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)]
) |>
  dplyr::left_join(boot_ci_r, by = "condition") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    r_aut = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", r_aut, ci_lo_aut, ci_hi_aut),
    r_com = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", r_com, ci_lo_com, ci_hi_com),
    r_rel = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", r_rel, ci_lo_rel, ci_hi_rel)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(condition, r_aut, r_com, r_rel)

tbl_rq2b_obs |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    condition = "Experimental Condition",
    r_aut     = TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
    r_com     = TABLE_LABEL_COM,
    r_rel     = TABLE_LABEL_REL
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "Pearson r"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 3L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1L, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates. ",
    "Values = Pearson r [95% bootstrap percentile CI] between surrogate-level mean LLM score and questionnaire subscale score. ",
    "R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```

### 8.2.1b Table Pearson Correlations r with SE and p values


```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-observed-r-full
#| echo: false

# 1. Extract SE from the bootstrap object (apply sd to the replicates)
se_vals <- apply(boot_r_rq2$t, 2, sd)
names(se_vals) <- names(boot_r_rq2$t0)

# 2. Build table, compute p-values, format each cell individually
tbl_rq2b_obs_display <- tibble::tibble(
  condition = CONDITIONS,
  r_aut  = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)],
  r_com  = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)],
  r_rel  = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)],
  se_aut = se_vals[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)],
  se_com = se_vals[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)],
  se_rel = se_vals[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)]
) |>
  dplyr::left_join(boot_ci_r, by = "condition") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    p_aut = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(r_aut / se_aut))),
    p_com = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(r_com / se_com))),
    p_rel = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(r_rel / se_rel)))
  ) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    condition,
    r_aut     = sprintf("%.2f", r_aut),
    se_aut    = sprintf("%.2f", se_aut),
    ci_lo_aut = sprintf("%.2f", ci_lo_aut),
    ci_hi_aut = sprintf("%.2f", ci_hi_aut),
    p_aut     = ifelse(p_aut < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", p_aut)),
    r_com     = sprintf("%.2f", r_com),
    se_com    = sprintf("%.2f", se_com),
    ci_lo_com = sprintf("%.2f", ci_lo_com),
    ci_hi_com = sprintf("%.2f", ci_hi_com),
    p_com     = ifelse(p_com < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", p_com)),
    r_rel     = sprintf("%.2f", r_rel),
    se_rel    = sprintf("%.2f", se_rel),
    ci_lo_rel = sprintf("%.2f", ci_lo_rel),
    ci_hi_rel = sprintf("%.2f", ci_hi_rel),
    p_rel     = ifelse(p_rel < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", p_rel))
  )

# 3. Render: base header = LL/UL at CI columns; add "95% CI" row above; add need row on top
tbl_rq2b_obs_display |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    condition = "Condition",
    r_aut = "Estimate", se_aut = "SE", ci_lo_aut = "LL", ci_hi_aut = "UL", p_aut = "p",
    r_com = "Estimate", se_com = "SE", ci_lo_com = "LL", ci_hi_com = "UL", p_com = "p",
    r_rel = "Estimate", se_rel = "SE", ci_lo_rel = "LL", ci_hi_rel = "UL", p_rel = "p"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p",
                      "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p",
                      "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L)
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1L, 5L, 5L, 5L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1:2, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), ". CIs are bootstrap percentile intervals (R = ",
    BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). ",
    "p-values are two-sided Wald tests using the bootstrap SE; ",
    "approximate and may be anti-conservative for |r| > .30."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```

### 8.2.1c Table Pearson Correlations r with 95% CI and p values

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-observed-r-no-se
#| echo: false

tbl_rq2b_obs_no_se <- tibble::tibble(
  condition = CONDITIONS,
  r_aut  = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)],
  r_com  = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)],
  r_rel  = boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)],
  se_aut = se_vals[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)],
  se_com = se_vals[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)],
  se_rel = se_vals[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)]
) |>
  dplyr::left_join(boot_ci_r, by = "condition") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    p_aut = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(r_aut / se_aut))),
    p_com = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(r_com / se_com))),
    p_rel = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(r_rel / se_rel)))
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    r_aut = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f], p %s",
                    r_aut, ci_lo_aut, ci_hi_aut,
                    ifelse(p_aut < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", p_aut))),
    r_com = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f], p %s",
                    r_com, ci_lo_com, ci_hi_com,
                    ifelse(p_com < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", p_com))),
    r_rel = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f], p %s",
                    r_rel, ci_lo_rel, ci_hi_rel,
                    ifelse(p_rel < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", p_rel)))
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(condition, r_aut, r_com, r_rel)

tbl_rq2b_obs_no_se |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    condition = "Condition",
    r_aut = TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
    r_com = TABLE_LABEL_COM,
    r_rel = TABLE_LABEL_REL
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), ". CIs are bootstrap percentile intervals (R = ",
    BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). ",
    "p-values are two-sided Wald tests using the bootstrap SE; ",
    "approximate and may be anti-conservative for |r| > .30."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-p-values
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Wald-test p-values (log\u2081\u2080 scale) for Pearson r by condition (RQ2b). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5); x-axis = target word length. Dashed = \u03b1 .05; dotted = \u03b1 .001."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

fig_rq2b_p_data <- tibble::tibble(
  condition   = CONDITIONS,
  Autonomy    = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)] / se_vals[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)]))),
  Competence  = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)] / se_vals[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)]))),
  Relatedness = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)] / se_vals[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)])))
) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(Autonomy, Competence, Relatedness),
                      names_to = "need", values_to = "p") |>
  dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need       = factor(need, levels = NEEDS),
    text_count = factor(text_count, levels = N_TEXTS_VARIANTS)
  )


fig_rq2b_p_values <- ggplot(fig_rq2b_p_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = p,
           group = text_count, color = text_count)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05,  linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.001, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    trans  = "log10",
    breaks = c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1),
    labels = c(".001", ".01", ".05", ".10", ".50", "1.00")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_TEXTS, name = "Number of texts") +
  labs(
    x = "Target word length",
    y = "p-value (log\u2081\u2080 scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.12, 0.2),
    legend.direction     = "vertical",
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq2b_p_values
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. p = 2 \u00d7 (1 \u2212 \u03a6(|r / SE|)), Wald test with bootstrap SE (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). Dashed line = \u03b1 .05; dotted line = \u03b1 .001.

### 8.2.2 r Matrix with Bootstrap CIs (per Need)

Full 3×3 matrix of *r* [95% bootstrap CI] per need. Bootstrap percentile CIs
from `boot_r_rq2` (run in `setup-rq2-bootstrap`, R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-r-matrices
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

for (need in NEEDS) {
  need_prefix <- switch(need,
    "Autonomy"    = "aut",
    "Competence"  = "com",
    "Relatedness" = "rel"
  )
  ft <- format_rq2_matrix(boot_r_rq2, need_prefix) |>
    flextable() |>
    style_apa7() |>
    set_header_labels(target_words = "Length") |>
    add_header_row(
      values    = c("", "Number of Texts (n)"),
      colwidths = c(1L, 3L)
    ) |>
    set_caption(paste("Table: Pearson r [95% Bootstrap CI] —", need)) |>
    add_apa_note(paste0(
      "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates. ",
      "Pearson r between surrogate-level mean LLM score and questionnaire subscale score. ",
      "Bootstrap percentile CIs, R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "."
    )) |>
    autofit()
  cat(knitr::knit_print(ft))
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

### 8.2.3 Line Plots: r by Condition per Need

#### Per Need

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-r-lines
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Pearson r (LLM score vs. questionnaire subscale score) by condition (RQ2b). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5); x-axis = target word length (fully independent). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

# Build long plot data across all 3 needs from boot_r_rq2
fig_rq2b_data <- purrr::map_dfr(
  list(
    list(prefix = "aut", label = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    list(prefix = "com", label = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    list(prefix = "rel", label = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  ),
  function(x) {
    col_idx <- paste0(x$prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
    boot_t  <- boot_r_rq2$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]
    tibble::tibble(
      condition = CONDITIONS,
      need      = x$label,
      r         = boot_r_rq2$t0[col_idx],
      ci_lo     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_hi     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) |>
      dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
      dplyr::mutate(text_count = factor(text_count, levels = N_TEXTS_VARIANTS))
  }
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
                                               TABLE_LABEL_COM,
                                               TABLE_LABEL_REL)))

fig_rq2b_lines <- ggplot(fig_rq2b_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = r,
           group = text_count, color = text_count)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60",
             linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, alpha = 0.7, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_TEXTS) +
  labs(
    title    = "RQ2b: LLM\u2013Questionnaire Correspondence by Condition",
    subtitle = paste0("N = ", nrow(wide_rq2),
                      " AI surrogates; error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = ",
                      BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ")"),
    x     = "Target word length",
    y     = "Pearson r",
    color = "Texts (n)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text            = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)
  )

fig_rq2b_lines
```

#### Shaded

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-r-lines-shaded
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Pearson r (LLM score vs. questionnaire subscale score) by condition (RQ2b). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5), shaded light to dark; x-axis = target word length (fully independent). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

fig_rq2b_r_lines_shaded <- ggplot(fig_rq2b_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = r,
           group = text_count, color = text_count, fill = text_count)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60",
             linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.12, color = NA, position = DODGE) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.2, linetype = "solid", position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  #coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.15, 0.3)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
  labs(
    x = "Target word length",
    y = "Pearson r"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.28, 0.15),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq2b_r_lines_shaded
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). Line shading light to dark = 1, 3, 5 texts.



#### Shaded v2

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-r-lines-v2
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Pearson r (LLM score vs. questionnaire subscale score) by condition (RQ2b). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5); x-axis = target word length (fully independent). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs. Dashed line = pooled OLS fit across all 9 conditions within each need panel (r ~ target_words, marginal)."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

fig_rq2b_r_lines_shaded <- ggplot(fig_rq2b_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = r,
           group = text_count, color = text_count, fill = text_count)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60",
             linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(x = target_words, y = r, group = 1),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    method      = "lm",
    se          = FALSE,
    color       = COL_HU_SHADES[4],
    linetype    = "dashed",
    linewidth   = 1.0
  ) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.2, linetype = "solid", position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
  labs(
    x = "Target word length",
    y = "Pearson r"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.28, 0.15),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq2b_r_lines_shaded
```

#### By Volume

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-r-by-volume
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Pearson r (LLM score vs. questionnaire subscale score) by total word volume (RQ2b). Lines = need dimension; x-axis = target words × text count; each point = one condition. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_rq2b_vol_data <- fig_rq2b_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(total_volume = as.integer(as.character(target_words)) * as.integer(as.character(text_count))) |>
  dplyr::arrange(need, total_volume)

pd_vol_r <- position_dodge(width = 30)

fig_rq2b_r_by_volume <- ggplot(fig_rq2b_vol_data,
       aes(x = total_volume, y = r, group = need, color = need)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60",
             linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, position = pd_vol_r) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, position = pd_vol_r) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, alpha = 0.7, position = pd_vol_r) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(fig_rq2b_vol_data$total_volume)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COL_AI_LLM) +
  labs(
    title    = "RQ2b: LLM\u2013Questionnaire Correspondence by Total Word Volume",
    subtitle = paste0("N = ", nrow(wide_rq2),
                      " AI surrogates; error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = ",
                      BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ")"),
    x     = "Total word volume (target words \u00d7 text count)",
    y     = "Pearson r",
    color = "Need"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text            = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

fig_rq2b_r_by_volume
```

#### Shaded, actual word count on x-axis

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-r-lines-shaded-by-actual-words
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Pearson r (LLM score vs. questionnaire subscale score) by condition (RQ2b). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5), shaded light to dark; x-axis = mean actual word count per condition. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

fig_rq2b_r_actual_data <- fig_rq2b_data |>
  dplyr::left_join(mean_actual_words, by = "condition")

fig_rq2b_r_lines_shaded_by_actual_words <- ggplot(fig_rq2b_r_actual_data,
       aes(x = mean_actual_words, y = r,
           group = text_count, color = text_count, fill = text_count)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60",
             linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.12, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.2, linetype = "solid") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1500),
    breaks = c(0, 50, 150, 250, 400, 750, 1250, 1500)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.15, 0.3)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Mean actual word count",
    y = "Pearson r"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x         = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.28, 0.15),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq2b_r_lines_shaded_by_actual_words
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). x-axis shows mean actual word count per condition; line shading light to dark = 1, 3, 5 texts.

### 8.2.3b Cross-Correlation Heat Matrix: LLM × Questionnaire per Condition

Each panel shows the full 6 × 6 Pearson correlation matrix between all three LLM-scored need dimensions and all three questionnaire dimensions, separately per experimental condition. The upper-left block captures within-LLM inter-need correlations; the lower-right block captures within-questionnaire inter-need correlations (condition-invariant, since Q scores are persona-level constants). The two off-diagonal blocks are the cross-construct correlations: the main diagonal of those blocks (LLM Aut × Q Aut, LLM Com × Q Com, LLM Rel × Q Rel) reflects convergent validity; the off-diagonal cells reflect discriminant validity. Darker blue = stronger positive correlation; grey diagonal = 1.0 (suppressed as uninformative).

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-crosscor-heatmap
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "6×6 cross-correlation matrix (LLM scores × questionnaire scores) per experimental condition (RQ2b). Upper-left block: within-LLM inter-need correlations. Lower-right block: within-questionnaire inter-need correlations (condition-invariant). Off-diagonal blocks: LLM × questionnaire cross-correlations (convergent = main diagonal; discriminant = off-diagonal). Grey diagonal = 1.0. Color scale: white = 0, dark red = strong negative correlation, dark blue = strong positive correlation. N = AI surrogates per condition."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-dpi: 300

VAR_LABELS_6 <- c("LLM Aut", "LLM Com", "LLM Rel", "Q Aut", "Q Com", "Q Rel")

heat_crosscor_data <- purrr::map_dfr(CONDITIONS, function(cond) {
  llm_cols <- paste0(c("llm_aut_m_", "llm_com_m_", "llm_rel_m_"), cond)
  q_cols   <- c("q_aut_mean", "q_com_mean", "q_rel_mean")

  mat <- cor(
    dplyr::select(wide_rq2, dplyr::all_of(c(llm_cols, q_cols))),
    use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
  )
  dimnames(mat) <- list(VAR_LABELS_6, VAR_LABELS_6)

  as.data.frame(as.table(mat), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) |>
    dplyr::rename(var_row = Var1, var_col = Var2, r = Freq) |>
    dplyr::mutate(condition = cond) |>
    dplyr::as_tibble()
}) |>
  dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    var_col     = factor(var_col, levels = VAR_LABELS_6),
    # Reversed so LLM vars appear at top of y-axis
    var_row_rev = factor(var_row, levels = rev(VAR_LABELS_6)),
    is_diag     = var_row == var_col,
    r_fill      = dplyr::if_else(is_diag, NA_real_, r),
    r_label     = dplyr::if_else(is_diag, "—", sprintf("%.2f", r)),
    txt_color   = dplyr::case_when(
      is_diag       ~ "grey55",
      abs(r) > 0.30 ~ "white",
      TRUE          ~ "black"
    ),
    target_f = factor(
      target_words,
      levels = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
      labels = c("Short (50w)", "Medium (150w)", "Long (300w)")
    ),
    text_f = factor(
      text_count,
      levels = c(1L, 3L, 5L),
      labels = c("1 text", "3 texts", "5 texts")
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(
    match(var_row, VAR_LABELS_6) >= match(as.character(var_col), VAR_LABELS_6)
  )

fig_rq2b_crosscor <- ggplot(heat_crosscor_data,
       aes(x = var_col, y = var_row_rev, fill = r_fill)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = r_label, color = txt_color),
    size = 2.4, family = THESIS_FONT
  ) +
  # Block separators between LLM (top 3 rows / left 3 cols) and Q (bottom 3 / right 3)
  geom_hline(yintercept = 3.5, color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 3.5, color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(target_f), cols = vars(text_f)) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = COL_HU_SHADES[1],   # darkest red — strong negative
    mid      = "white",
    high     = COL_AI_SHADES[1],   # darkest blue — strong positive
    midpoint = 0,
    limits   = c(-0.6, 0.6),
    na.value = "grey93",           # diagonal cells
    name     = "Pearson r"
  ) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text             = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid       = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "plain"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm")
  )

fig_rq2b_crosscor
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-crosscor-heatmap
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Cross-Correlation Matrix with 95% CIs: All 6×6 Lower-Triangle Pairs per Condition (RQ2b)"

VAR_LABELS_6 <- c("LLM Aut", "LLM Com", "LLM Rel", "Q Aut", "Q Com", "Q Rel")

# 15 lower-triangle pairs; block: 1=within-LLM, 2=LLM×Q, 3=within-Q
pair_meta <- tidyr::expand_grid(
  row_i = seq_along(VAR_LABELS_6),
  col_i = seq_along(VAR_LABELS_6)
) |>
  dplyr::filter(row_i > col_i) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    row_var      = VAR_LABELS_6[row_i],
    col_var      = VAR_LABELS_6[col_i],
    col_nm       = sprintf("p%02d", dplyr::row_number()),
    block_order  = dplyr::case_when(
      row_i <= 3L               ~ 1L,
      row_i >= 4L & col_i >= 4L ~ 3L,
      TRUE                      ~ 2L
    ),
    # short header labels (row × col)
    hdr = dplyr::case_when(
      block_order == 1L ~ paste0(sub("LLM ", "", row_var), "×", sub("LLM ", "", col_var)),
      block_order == 3L ~ paste0(sub("Q ", "Q.", row_var), "×", sub("Q ", "Q.", col_var)),
      TRUE              ~ paste0(sub("Q ", "Q.", row_var), "×", sub("LLM ", "L.", col_var))
    ),
    is_convergent = row_i - col_i == 3L  # QAut×LAut, QCom×LCom, QRel×LRel
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(block_order, row_i, col_i)

# Fisher z CI helper — standard for Pearson r intervals
fisher_fmt <- function(r, n, ci_level = CI_LEVEL) {
  z     <- atanh(r)
  se_z  <- 1 / sqrt(n - 3L)
  crit  <- qnorm((1 + ci_level) / 2)
  sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", r, tanh(z - crit * se_z), tanh(z + crit * se_z))
}

# One row per condition
cond_order <- condition_lookup |>
  dplyr::arrange(target_words, text_count) |>
  dplyr::pull(condition)

crosscor_rows <- purrr::map_dfr(cond_order, function(cond) {
  lu       <- condition_lookup[condition_lookup$condition == cond, ]
  llm_cols <- paste0(c("llm_aut_m_", "llm_com_m_", "llm_rel_m_"), cond)
  q_cols   <- c("q_aut_mean", "q_com_mean", "q_rel_mean")
  all_cols <- c(llm_cols, q_cols)
  df       <- dplyr::select(wide_rq2, dplyr::all_of(all_cols))
  n        <- sum(complete.cases(df))
  m        <- cor(df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  dimnames(m) <- list(VAR_LABELS_6, VAR_LABELS_6)

  cells <- setNames(
    purrr::map_chr(seq_len(nrow(pair_meta)), function(k)
      fisher_fmt(m[pair_meta$row_var[k], pair_meta$col_var[k]], n)),
    pair_meta$col_nm
  )
  tibble::tibble(
    Condition = paste0(
      dplyr::case_when(lu$target_words == 50L ~ "Short (50w)",
                       lu$target_words == 150L ~ "Medium (150w)",
                       TRUE ~ "Long (300w)"),
      " × ", lu$text_count,
      dplyr::if_else(lu$text_count == 1L, " text", " texts")
    )
  ) |>
    dplyr::bind_cols(as.data.frame(t(cells), stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
})

# Column positions (1-based; col 1 = Condition)
n_within_llm <- sum(pair_meta$block_order == 1L)   # 3
n_cross      <- sum(pair_meta$block_order == 2L)   # 9
n_within_q   <- sum(pair_meta$block_order == 3L)   # 3
conv_j       <- which(pair_meta$is_convergent) + 1L

hdr_values <- setNames(
  as.list(c("Condition", pair_meta$hdr)),
  c("Condition", pair_meta$col_nm)
)

tbl_rq2b_crosscor <- flextable::flextable(crosscor_rows) |>
  flextable::set_header_labels(values = hdr_values) |>
  flextable::add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "Within LLM", "LLM × Questionnaire", "Within Q"),
    colwidths = c(1L, n_within_llm, n_cross, n_within_q)
  ) |>
  flextable::align(i = 1L, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  flextable::align(i = 2L, j = 2:16, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  flextable::align(j = 2:16, align = "center", part = "body") |>
  flextable::bold(j = conv_j, part = "body") |>
  flextable::vline(
    j      = c(1L + n_within_llm, 1L + n_within_llm + n_cross),
    border = officer::fp_border(color = "grey60", width = 0.75),
    part   = "body"
  )

tbl_rq2b_crosscor |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates. ",
    "Values = Pearson r [95% CI]; CIs are Fisher-z intervals. ",
    "Note: main r-tables (§8.2.1) report bootstrap percentile CIs with Wald-test p-values; ",
    "both methods are reported and yield similar intervals at these sample sizes. ",
    "Within LLM: inter-need correlations between LLM-scored dimensions ",
    "(A× = Aut, C× = Com, R× = Rel). ",
    "LLM × Questionnaire: cross-construct correlations; Q. = questionnaire subscale, L. = LLM dimension; ",
    "bold = convergent validity (same need dimension). ",
    "Within Q: inter-need correlations between questionnaire subscales (condition-invariant)."
  )) |>
  flextable::autofit()
```

### 8.2.3c Spearman Cross-Correlation Matrix

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-crosscor-heatmap-spearman
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "6×6 cross-correlation matrix (Spearman ρ, LLM scores × questionnaire scores) per experimental condition (RQ2b). Layout identical to Pearson version; values are rank-based. Upper-left block: within-LLM inter-need correlations. Lower-right block: within-questionnaire inter-need correlations (condition-invariant). Off-diagonal blocks: LLM × questionnaire cross-correlations (convergent = main diagonal; discriminant = off-diagonal). Grey diagonal = 1.0. N = AI surrogates per condition."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-dpi: 300

heat_crosscor_data_spearman <- purrr::map_dfr(CONDITIONS, function(cond) {
  llm_cols <- paste0(c("llm_aut_m_", "llm_com_m_", "llm_rel_m_"), cond)
  q_cols   <- c("q_aut_mean", "q_com_mean", "q_rel_mean")

  mat <- cor(
    dplyr::select(wide_rq2, dplyr::all_of(c(llm_cols, q_cols))),
    use    = "pairwise.complete.obs",
    method = "spearman"
  )
  dimnames(mat) <- list(VAR_LABELS_6, VAR_LABELS_6)

  as.data.frame(as.table(mat), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) |>
    dplyr::rename(var_row = Var1, var_col = Var2, r = Freq) |>
    dplyr::mutate(condition = cond) |>
    dplyr::as_tibble()
}) |>
  dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    var_col     = factor(var_col, levels = VAR_LABELS_6),
    var_row_rev = factor(var_row, levels = rev(VAR_LABELS_6)),
    is_diag     = var_row == var_col,
    r_fill      = dplyr::if_else(is_diag, NA_real_, r),
    r_label     = dplyr::if_else(is_diag, "—", sprintf("%.2f", r)),
    txt_color   = dplyr::case_when(
      is_diag       ~ "grey55",
      abs(r) > 0.30 ~ "white",
      TRUE          ~ "black"
    ),
    target_f = factor(
      target_words,
      levels = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
      labels = c("Short (50w)", "Medium (150w)", "Long (300w)")
    ),
    text_f = factor(
      text_count,
      levels = c(1L, 3L, 5L),
      labels = c("1 text", "3 texts", "5 texts")
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(
    match(var_row, VAR_LABELS_6) >= match(as.character(var_col), VAR_LABELS_6)
  )

fig_rq2b_crosscor_spearman <- ggplot(heat_crosscor_data_spearman,
       aes(x = var_col, y = var_row_rev, fill = r_fill)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = r_label, color = txt_color),
    size = 2.4, family = THESIS_FONT
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 3.5, color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 3.5, color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(target_f), cols = vars(text_f)) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = COL_HU_SHADES[1],
    mid      = "white",
    high     = COL_AI_SHADES[1],
    midpoint = 0,
    limits   = c(-0.6, 0.6),
    na.value = "grey93",
    name     = "Spearman ρ"
  ) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text             = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid       = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "plain"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm")
  )

fig_rq2b_crosscor_spearman
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-crosscor-heatmap-spearman
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Cross-Correlation Matrix with 95% CIs (Spearman ρ): All 6×6 Lower-Triangle Pairs per Condition (RQ2b)"

# pair_meta, VAR_LABELS_6, fisher_fmt, cond_order, hdr_values defined in tbl-rq2b-crosscor-heatmap

crosscor_rows_spearman <- purrr::map_dfr(cond_order, function(cond) {
  lu       <- condition_lookup[condition_lookup$condition == cond, ]
  llm_cols <- paste0(c("llm_aut_m_", "llm_com_m_", "llm_rel_m_"), cond)
  q_cols   <- c("q_aut_mean", "q_com_mean", "q_rel_mean")
  all_cols <- c(llm_cols, q_cols)
  df       <- dplyr::select(wide_rq2, dplyr::all_of(all_cols))
  n        <- sum(complete.cases(df))
  m        <- cor(df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
  dimnames(m) <- list(VAR_LABELS_6, VAR_LABELS_6)

  cells <- setNames(
    purrr::map_chr(seq_len(nrow(pair_meta)), function(k)
      fisher_fmt(m[pair_meta$row_var[k], pair_meta$col_var[k]], n)),
    pair_meta$col_nm
  )
  tibble::tibble(
    Condition = paste0(
      dplyr::case_when(lu$target_words == 50L ~ "Short (50w)",
                       lu$target_words == 150L ~ "Medium (150w)",
                       TRUE ~ "Long (300w)"),
      " × ", lu$text_count,
      dplyr::if_else(lu$text_count == 1L, " text", " texts")
    )
  ) |>
    dplyr::bind_cols(as.data.frame(t(cells), stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
})

tbl_rq2b_crosscor_spearman <- flextable::flextable(crosscor_rows_spearman) |>
  flextable::set_header_labels(values = hdr_values) |>
  flextable::add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "Within LLM", "LLM × Questionnaire", "Within Q"),
    colwidths = c(1L, n_within_llm, n_cross, n_within_q)
  ) |>
  flextable::align(i = 1L, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  flextable::align(i = 2L, j = 2:16, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  flextable::align(j = 2:16, align = "center", part = "body") |>
  flextable::bold(j = conv_j, part = "body") |>
  flextable::vline(
    j      = c(1L + n_within_llm, 1L + n_within_llm + n_cross),
    border = officer::fp_border(color = "grey60", width = 0.75),
    part   = "body"
  )

tbl_rq2b_crosscor_spearman |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates. ",
    "Values = Spearman ρ [95% CI]; CIs are Fisher-z intervals (approximate for ρ). ",
    "Within LLM: inter-need correlations between LLM-scored dimensions ",
    "(A× = Aut, C× = Com, R× = Rel). ",
    "LLM × Questionnaire: cross-construct correlations; Q. = questionnaire subscale, L. = LLM dimension; ",
    "bold = convergent validity (same need dimension). ",
    "Within Q: inter-need correlations between questionnaire subscales (condition-invariant)."
  )) |>
  flextable::autofit()
```

### 8.2.4 Bootstrap Contrasts

7 pre-specified endpoint contrasts per need. Across-length contrasts use fully
independent texts (strongest evidence). Across-n_texts contrasts reflect
cumulative structure; CIs are correlated observations — state explicitly.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-contrasts
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Bootstrap Contrasts \u0394r [95% CI] per Need"

format_rq2_contrasts(boot_r_rq2, "aut", delta_label = TABLE_LABEL_AUT) |>
  dplyr::rename(!!TABLE_LABEL_AUT := 2) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    format_rq2_contrasts(boot_r_rq2, "com", delta_label = TABLE_LABEL_COM) |>
      dplyr::rename(!!TABLE_LABEL_COM := 2),
    by = "Contrast"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    format_rq2_contrasts(boot_r_rq2, "rel", delta_label = TABLE_LABEL_REL) |>
      dplyr::rename(!!TABLE_LABEL_REL := 2),
    by = "Contrast"
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "\u0394r [95% Bootstrap CI]"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 3L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "\u0394r = r\u2081 \u2212 r\u2082. Bootstrap percentile CIs, R = ",
    BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ". ",
    "Across-n_texts contrasts reflect cumulative text structure; CIs are correlated observations."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```


```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-contrasts-full
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Bootstrap Contrasts Δr with SE and p-Values per Need"

build_contrast_need <- function(prefix) {
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_r_rq2, prefix) |>
    dplyr::rename_with(~ paste0(., "_", prefix), -Contrast)
}

build_contrast_need("aut") |>
  dplyr::left_join(build_contrast_need("com"), by = "Contrast") |>
  dplyr::left_join(build_contrast_need("rel"), by = "Contrast") |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    Contrast     = "Contrast",
    Estimate_aut = "Estimate", SE_aut = "SE", LL_aut = "LL", UL_aut = "UL", p_aut = "p",
    Estimate_com = "Estimate", SE_com = "SE", LL_com = "LL", UL_com = "UL", p_com = "p",
    Estimate_rel = "Estimate", SE_rel = "SE", LL_rel = "LL", UL_rel = "UL", p_rel = "p"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p",
                      "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p",
                      "Estimate", "SE", "95% CI", "p"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 1L)
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    top = TRUE,
    values    = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1L, 5L, 5L, 5L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1:2, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Δr = r₁ − r₂. SE = bootstrap SD of the contrast distribution. ",
    "p = Wald test (2×Φ(−|Δr/SE|)). ",
    "95% CI = bootstrap percentile interval, R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ". ",
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates. ",
    "Across-n_texts contrasts reflect cumulative text structure; CIs are correlated observations."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-contrasts-forest
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: "Bootstrap contrast estimates (Δr) with 95% CIs per need dimension (RQ2b). Each row = one pre-specified endpoint contrast; x-axis = Δr; lines = 95% bootstrap percentile CI; dashed vertical line = 0. Dotted horizontal lines separate contrast groups (length effects, text-count effects, grand diagonal)."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

rq2b_contrast_plot_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_r_rq2, "aut") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_r_rq2, "com") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
  format_rq2_contrasts_full(boot_r_rq2, "rel") |> dplyr::mutate(need = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need     = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = rev(unique(Contrast)))
  )

ggplot(rq2b_contrast_plot_data,
       aes(x = Estimate, y = Contrast)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(1.5, 4.5), linetype = "dotted", color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = LL, xend = UL, y = Contrast, yend = Contrast), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  labs(
    x = "Δr [95% bootstrap CI]",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.2)
  )
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Bootstrap percentile CIs, R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`. Groups (top to bottom): length effects (Δr across word length at fixed text count); text-count effects (Δr across text count at fixed length); grand diagonal.

### 8.2.5 Robustness check: Spearman ρ

Spearman's ρ as a fallback check

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-spearman-check
#| include: true

# Compute Spearman rho for all 9 conditions × 3 needs as a robustness check.
# Compare to Pearson r in boot_r_rq2$t0. If substantively different, flag.
spearman_check <- tibble::tibble(
  cond   = CONDITIONS,
  rho_aut = vapply(aut_cols,
    function(col) cor(wide_rq2[[col]], wide_rq2$q_aut_mean,
                      method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"), numeric(1L)),
  rho_com = vapply(com_cols,
    function(col) cor(wide_rq2[[col]], wide_rq2$q_com_mean,
                      method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"), numeric(1L)),
  rho_rel = vapply(rel_cols,
    function(col) cor(wide_rq2[[col]], wide_rq2$q_rel_mean,
                      method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"), numeric(1L)),
  r_aut   = round(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)], 3L),
  r_com   = round(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)], 3L),
  r_rel   = round(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)], 3L)
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    diff_aut = round(rho_aut - r_aut, 3L),
    diff_com = round(rho_com - r_com, 3L),
    diff_rel = round(rho_rel - r_rel, 3L)
  )

# Flag conditions where Spearman and Pearson differ by more than 0.05
large_diff <- spearman_check |>
  dplyr::filter(abs(diff_aut) > 0.05 | abs(diff_com) > 0.05 | abs(diff_rel) > 0.05)

if (nrow(large_diff) > 0) {
  warning("Spearman/Pearson divergence > .05 in some conditions — review robustness.")
  print(large_diff)
} else {
  cat("Robustness check OK: Spearman and Pearson r agree within .05 across all conditions.\n")
}
```


### 8.2.6 Spearman Correlation

Spearman ρ mirrors §8.2.3 but uses rank-based correlation. Bootstrap CIs use the same resampling scheme as `boot_r_rq2`.

```{r}
#| label: boot-rho-rq2b
#| include: false
#| cache: true
#| cache.extra: !expr list(file.mtime(data_path), BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)

boot_fn_rho_rq2b <- function(data, indices) {
  d <- data[indices, ]
  c(
    setNames(
      vapply(aut_cols,
             function(col) cor(d[[col]], d$q_aut_mean,
                               method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"),
             numeric(1L)),
      paste0("aut_", CONDITIONS)
    ),
    setNames(
      vapply(com_cols,
             function(col) cor(d[[col]], d$q_com_mean,
                               method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"),
             numeric(1L)),
      paste0("com_", CONDITIONS)
    ),
    setNames(
      vapply(rel_cols,
             function(col) cor(d[[col]], d$q_rel_mean,
                               method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"),
             numeric(1L)),
      paste0("rel_", CONDITIONS)
    )
  )
}

boot_rho_rq2b <- boot::boot(data = wide_rq2_df, statistic = boot_fn_rho_rq2b,
                             R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
colnames(boot_rho_rq2b$t) <- names(boot_rho_rq2b$t0)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq2b-rho-lines-shaded
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Spearman ρ (LLM score vs. questionnaire subscale score) by condition (RQ2b). Lines = number of texts (1, 3, 5), shaded light to dark; x-axis = target word length (fully independent). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 400

fig_rq2b_rho_data <- purrr::map_dfr(
  list(
    list(prefix = "aut", label = TABLE_LABEL_AUT),
    list(prefix = "com", label = TABLE_LABEL_COM),
    list(prefix = "rel", label = TABLE_LABEL_REL)
  ),
  function(x) {
    col_idx <- paste0(x$prefix, "_", CONDITIONS)
    boot_t  <- boot_rho_rq2b$t[, col_idx, drop = FALSE]
    tibble::tibble(
      condition = CONDITIONS,
      need      = x$label,
      r         = boot_rho_rq2b$t0[col_idx],
      ci_lo     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_LO, na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_hi     = apply(boot_t, 2L, quantile, CI_Q_HI, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) |>
      dplyr::left_join(condition_lookup, by = "condition") |>
      dplyr::mutate(text_count = factor(text_count, levels = N_TEXTS_VARIANTS))
  }
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
                                               TABLE_LABEL_COM,
                                               TABLE_LABEL_REL)))

ggplot(fig_rq2b_rho_data,
       aes(x = target_words, y = r,
           group = text_count, color = text_count, fill = text_count)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60",
             linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), alpha = 0.12, color = NA, position = DODGE) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.2, linetype = "solid", position = DODGE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8, position = DODGE) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8, position = DODGE) +
  facet_wrap(~need, ncol = 3L) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(50L, 150L, 300L),
    labels = c("Short\n(50)", "Medium\n(150)", "Long\n(300)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = COL_AI_TEXTS,
    name   = "Number of texts"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
  labs(
    x = "Target word length",
    y = "Spearman ρ"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "plain", colour = "black", size = THESIS_FONTSIZE),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position    = c(0.28, 0.15),
    legend.direction   = "horizontal",
    legend.margin      = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key         = element_blank(),
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "plain")
  )
```

*Note.* N = `r nrow(wide_rq2)` AI surrogates. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). Line shading light to dark = 1, 3, 5 texts.


# 9 Sensitivity: Polychoric Correlations on Rounded Scores (AI Surrogates)

Polychoric correlation treats both variables as ordinal (discretized underlying
latent continua), which is more appropriate than Pearson *r* when scores are
rounded integers on a 1–5 scale. Both LLM and questionnaire scores are rounded
to the nearest integer before computing polychoric *r*. The 95% CIs are
bootstrap percentile (R = 1,000; polychoric is computationally heavier than
Pearson so a smaller *B* is used here). Δr shows the difference from the
unrounded Pearson *r* (@tbl-rq2b-observed-r).

This analysis covers **AI surrogates only** across all 9 experimental conditions
(3 text lengths × 3 text counts).

```{r}
#| label: sec9-polychoric-rq2
#| include: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| cache: true
#| cache.extra: !expr list(file.mtime(data_path), BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)

POLY_BOOT_R_RQ2 <- 1000L  # polychoric is slower; 1000 sufficient for sensitivity

poly_boot_ci_rq2 <- function(data, llm_col, q_col) {
  df    <- data[!is.na(data[[llm_col]]) & !is.na(data[[q_col]]), ]
  x_hat <- round(df[[llm_col]])
  y_hat <- round(df[[q_col]])
  r_hat <- as.numeric(psych::polychoric(cbind(x_hat, y_hat))$rho[1, 2])
  n     <- nrow(df)
  boot_rs <- replicate(POLY_BOOT_R_RQ2, {
    i <- sample.int(n, replace = TRUE)
    tryCatch(
      as.numeric(psych::polychoric(cbind(round(df[[llm_col]][i]),
                                         round(df[[q_col]][i])))$rho[1, 2]),
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    )
  })
  boot_rs <- boot_rs[!is.na(boot_rs)]
  list(r     = r_hat,
       ci_lo = unname(quantile(boot_rs, CI_Q_LO)),
       ci_hi = unname(quantile(boot_rs, CI_Q_HI)))
}

fmt_poly_rq2 <- function(x) sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", x$r, x$ci_lo, x$ci_hi)

set.seed(2026)
poly_results_rq2 <- lapply(CONDITIONS, function(cond) {
  list(
    condition = cond,
    aut = poly_boot_ci_rq2(wide_rq2_df, paste0("llm_aut_m_", cond), "q_aut_mean"),
    com = poly_boot_ci_rq2(wide_rq2_df, paste0("llm_com_m_", cond), "q_com_mean"),
    rel = poly_boot_ci_rq2(wide_rq2_df, paste0("llm_rel_m_", cond), "q_rel_mean")
  )
})
names(poly_results_rq2) <- CONDITIONS

poly_tbl_rq2 <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(poly_results_rq2, function(x) {
  r_p_aut <- unname(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("aut_", x$condition)])
  r_p_com <- unname(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("com_", x$condition)])
  r_p_rel <- unname(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("rel_", x$condition)])
  data.frame(
    condition  = x$condition,
    r_poly_aut = fmt_poly_rq2(x$aut),
    delta_aut  = round(x$aut$r - r_p_aut, 2),
    r_poly_com = fmt_poly_rq2(x$com),
    delta_com  = round(x$com$r - r_p_com, 2),
    r_poly_rel = fmt_poly_rq2(x$rel),
    delta_rel  = round(x$rel$r - r_p_rel, 2),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq2b-polychoric
#| tbl-cap: "Polychoric Correlations Between Rounded LLM and Questionnaire Scores per Condition and Need (AI Surrogates — Ordinal Sensitivity Check)"
#| echo: false

poly_tbl_rq2 |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(j = c("delta_aut", "delta_com", "delta_rel"), digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    condition  = "Experimental Condition",
    r_poly_aut = "r_poly [95% CI]",
    delta_aut  = "Δr",
    r_poly_com = "r_poly [95% CI]",
    delta_com  = "Δr",
    r_poly_rel = "r_poly [95% CI]",
    delta_rel  = "Δr"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    colwidths = c(1L, 2L, 2L, 2L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1L, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "N = ", nrow(wide_rq2), " AI surrogates. ",
    "Polychoric correlation between surrogate-level mean LLM score and questionnaire subscale score, ",
    "both rounded to the nearest integer. ",
    "95% CIs: bootstrap percentile (R = ", POLY_BOOT_R_RQ2, "). ",
    "Δr = r_poly − r_Pearson (unrounded; see @tbl-rq2b-observed-r)."
  )) |>
  autofit()
```

```{r}
#| label: sec9-cleanup
#| include: false

#rm(poly_tbl_rq2)
# poly_results_rq2, poly_boot_ci_rq2, fmt_poly_rq2, POLY_BOOT_R_RQ2 kept alive for section 10
```

# 10 Summary: Pearson, Spearman, and Polychoric Correlations (AI Surrogates)

```{r}
#| label: sec10-method-comparison-rq2
#| include: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| cache: true
#| cache.extra: !expr list(file.mtime(data_path), BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)

# Spearman bootstrap CIs for all 9 conditions × 3 needs (R = POLY_BOOT_R_RQ2).
# Pearson r + CIs are reused from boot_r_rq2 / boot_ci_r (section 8).
# Polychoric r + CIs are reused from poly_results_rq2 (section 9).

boot_fn_spearman_single <- function(data, i) {
  cor(data$llm[i], data$q[i], method = "spearman")
}

set.seed(2026)
spearman_results_rq2 <- lapply(CONDITIONS, function(cond) {
  make_pair <- function(llm_col, q_col) {
    df <- data.frame(llm = wide_rq2_df[[llm_col]], q = wide_rq2_df[[q_col]])
    df[complete.cases(df), ]
  }
  pa <- make_pair(paste0("llm_aut_m_", cond), "q_aut_mean")
  pc <- make_pair(paste0("llm_com_m_", cond), "q_com_mean")
  pr <- make_pair(paste0("llm_rel_m_", cond), "q_rel_mean")
  ba <- boot::boot(pa, boot_fn_spearman_single, R = POLY_BOOT_R_RQ2)
  bc <- boot::boot(pc, boot_fn_spearman_single, R = POLY_BOOT_R_RQ2)
  br <- boot::boot(pr, boot_fn_spearman_single, R = POLY_BOOT_R_RQ2)
  ca <- boot::boot.ci(ba, type = "perc")
  cc <- boot::boot.ci(bc, type = "perc")
  cr <- boot::boot.ci(br, type = "perc")
  list(
    condition   = cond,
    rho_aut     = ba$t0, rho_aut_lo = ca$percent[4], rho_aut_hi = ca$percent[5],
    rho_com     = bc$t0, rho_com_lo = cc$percent[4], rho_com_hi = cc$percent[5],
    rho_rel     = br$t0, rho_rel_lo = cr$percent[4], rho_rel_hi = cr$percent[5]
  )
})
names(spearman_results_rq2) <- CONDITIONS

fmt_ci_rq2 <- function(r, lo, hi) sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", r, lo, hi)

method_tbl_rq2 <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(CONDITIONS, function(cond) {
  sp    <- spearman_results_rq2[[cond]]
  po    <- poly_results_rq2[[cond]]
  r_aut <- unname(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("aut_", cond)])
  r_com <- unname(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("com_", cond)])
  r_rel <- unname(boot_r_rq2$t0[paste0("rel_", cond)])
  ci    <- boot_ci_r[boot_ci_r$condition == cond, ]
  data.frame(
    condition    = cond,
    pearson_aut  = fmt_ci_rq2(r_aut, ci$ci_lo_aut, ci$ci_hi_aut),
    spearman_aut = fmt_ci_rq2(sp$rho_aut, sp$rho_aut_lo, sp$rho_aut_hi),
    poly_aut     = fmt_ci_rq2(po$aut$r, po$aut$ci_lo, po$aut$ci_hi),
    pearson_com  = fmt_ci_rq2(r_com, ci$ci_lo_com, ci$ci_hi_com),
    spearman_com = fmt_ci_rq2(sp$rho_com, sp$rho_com_lo, sp$rho_com_hi),
    poly_com     = fmt_ci_rq2(po$com$r, po$com$ci_lo, po$com$ci_hi),
    pearson_rel  = fmt_ci_rq2(r_rel, ci$ci_lo_rel, ci$ci_hi_rel),
    spearman_rel = fmt_ci_rq2(sp$rho_rel, sp$rho_rel_lo, sp$rho_rel_hi),
    poly_rel     = fmt_ci_rq2(po$rel$r, po$rel$ci_lo, po$rel$ci_hi),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

method_plot_data_rq2 <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(CONDITIONS, function(cond) {
```
